programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a Navy commander's wife, determining whether it is linked to her role as a therapist, or an act of terrorism since her husband's name has appeared on a Jihadist's target list. Elsewhere, the group discusses Halloween costumes, as well as Tony's recent dating habits. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team uncovers a fraudulent charity that preys on those searching for missing military personnel while investigating the murder of a retired Master Sergeant. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the cause of a hit-and-run incident in which a Petty Officer has been killed. Meanwhile, Gibbs works to clear a Navy Hospital Corpsman who has been indicted for illegally providing medical aid to victims of the crash, despite the injured insisting her techniques helped save their lives. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An enigmatic killer shoots dead a businessman spending Thanksgiving at a hotel in the city, then takes his glasses, jewellery and ID to Washington Dulles airport, which has been brought to a stand-still by a massive snowstorm. As the murderer's actions take effect, a terror alert is issued, leaving all northern US airports in a state of panic, and leaving it up to DiNozzo, Bishop and her husband - all of whom are trapped at Dulles by the weather - to bring the armed villain to justice. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherley(n)

programme.title: 48 Hours: Mystery

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: The decomposing body of a young woman is found in the bathroom of a New York hotel, having been undisturbed for three days. When Avery Ryan's and her team investigate, they discover the victim seemed to have been ordering room service three days after her death, and her messages to staff had released vicious malware that eradicated several hours of surveillance footage from the hotel's security cameras. The chain of evidence leads Ryan to a hacker thought to have been on-site at the time of the woman's death, but as Nelson and Ramirez continue digging for clues, it becomes clear this case is far from closed. Patricia Arquette stars(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Avery Ryan's old friend Jordan Tan seeks her help in locating his missing teenage daughter Zoey. When the team looks into high-schooler's online history, they discover she had been the target of hacks, faked phone calls and social media posts, as well as a malicious website titled 'Kill Yourself Zoey Tan'. The spate of abuse bears all the hallmarks of a particularly nasty case of online bullying, but Zoey's digital footprint offers the investigators a lead in the form of online boyfriend Owen, who lives 300 miles away and seems to have invited her to visit. However, when the team ventures to Owen's home, they discover his invitation was just another facet of the hate campaign, and with Zoey's whereabout still unknown, serious concerns are raised about her wellbeing. Crime drama, starring Patricia Arquette, with guests Byron Mann and BooBoo Stewart(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The dodgy dealer is asked to furnish a restaurant, but the items he acquires turn out to be stolen. To make matters worse, the owners turn up for the establishment's opening night. John Wells guest stars, with Ian McShane(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The antiques dealer is asked to track down an itinerant painter who holds the key to a shameful secret. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane(n)

programme.desc: Despatched by Munro to kill a French colonel, Sharpe is wounded in battle and hovers at death's door - but Harper and a mute nun nurse him back to health. programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Bomber learns his daughter has run away, and immediately flies home to lend his wife moral support - unaware the young lady in question is winging her way to Dusseldorf to be with him. Comedy drama, starring Pat Roach, Tim Healy, Gary Holton and Jimmy Nail(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Brenda makes it clear that she thinks Neville is living it up in Germany just as a leather-clad local girl, whom he merely shared a taxi with, accuses him of assaulting her. Cult comedy drama, starring Kevin Whately, Tim Healy and Julia Tobin(n)

programme.title: Ms Fisher's Modern Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Spin-off from Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries, set in the 1960s, following Miss Fisher's niece, who sets out to investigate what happened to her aunt. In the first of a two-part story, Peregrine Fisher arrives in Melbourne to collect her inheritance from her aunt, Phryne Fisher. Wanting to be part of the same Adventuresses' Club as her aunt, Peregrine decides to follow in Phryne's footsteps and solve the murder of a dead model at a bridal fashion show. Geraldine Hakewill stars(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance of an old friend leads Father Brown and Sid on a quest to find the mythical Lannington Rosary, and an old adversary, seemingly back from the dead, must be trusted to help. Mark Williams stars(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Armed only with a telescope, the priest hopes that a broken leg will not prevent him from solving the mystery surrounding the death of a member of Kembleford's Women's Institute. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams, Sorcha Cusack, Nancy Carroll and Tom Chambers(n)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: A groom-to-be is shot dead while on a hunting excursion during his stag trip. Though apparently an accident, none of his accompanying friends will admit to firing the bullet. Neill Rea stars(n)

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Julia refuses to accept the blame when one of her patients dies after surgery, and her investigations reveal an unusual pattern of deaths among alcoholics on the ward(n)

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Fantasy drama, starring John Simm as a detective who ends up in a coma while pursuing a serial killer - and when he comes round, he finds he has somehow been transported back to 1973. As he attempts to uncover what has happened to him, he must also contend with the macho behaviour of his Sweeneyesque colleagues, as well as a murderer whose crimes are oddly similar to the one he was investigating in his own time(n)

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam learns Gene has planted evidence on a notorious armed robber, and decides to let him go. However, the crook then continues his crime spree, critically wounding a cleaner from the police station in the process. The idealistic detective begins to question whether his colleague's approach might be justified - but fears his doubts could affect his chances of returning to the 21st century(n)

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: The beleaguered barrister puts aside his domestic problems to defend a prostitute on the charge of murdering a high-profile businessman - a tricky case made all the more difficult by the discovery that she has many enemies, all of them determined to blacken her character. Drama, starring John Thaw(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Jack and Strickland form an unlikely alliance that sees them break the rules to find out why a British soldier died while taking part in a Ministry of Defence medical trial. With three of the man's colleagues now suffering from psychological, drink or memory problems, the team considers whether he died as a result of the experiment, or if he was a victim of a cover-up by the Army(n)

programme.title: Mansfield Park
programme.desc: Part two of six. While Sir Thomas is overseas, a prosperous family arrives in the neighbourhood. Jane Austen adaptation, starring Sylvestra Le Touzel(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Rob probes the circumstances of Jimmy Cross's shooting and learns the truth about his father's murder, sending him on a mission of vengeance that culminates in a tense stand-off. Elsewhere, Abi's kidnapper sends Samantha a macabre message that leads her to an equally sinister website, and Phil taunts Neil over his affair with Andrea(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The hunt for Abi Nixon's kidnapper takes on renewed urgency when it appears she is being held captive in a sealed container with only a couple of hours' oxygen left. Psychological profiler Dr Hugh Wallis reckons the taunting messages sent by the culprit point to an old adversary with a grudge, and soon pinpoints a likely suspect, while Phil offers the distraught Sam solace in her darkest hour(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Samantha is appalled when it becomes clear that psychological profiler Dr Hugh Wallis has brainwashed Abi, who not only refuses to name him as her kidnapper but agrees to return home with him. The situation becomes even more dire when Peter Baxter is charged with the abduction, leaving Samantha wondering how she can convince her colleagues of the terrible truth. Meanwhile, sexy new recruit PC Amber Johannsen turns heads at Sun Hill, although Gina is unimpressed by her slipshod approach to police work, and Ramani crosses swords with an abusive wife and mother(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jim makes a partial confession to June about his debts, leaving her in no mood to enjoy her retirement party, preferring to drown her sorrows rather than facing up to the reality of her husband's betrayal. As the booze flows freely, Amber makes a bee-line for Steve and promptly seduces the virginal copper - doubtless setting the scene for plenty of emotional tempests to come. By contrast, Ramani's professionalism impresses Jack so much he puts her in charge of a new Vulnerable Persons Unit(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The local Blainthorp Music Festival celebrates its 50th birthday, bringing with it fierce competition. The sleuth is called in to investigate why competitors have mysteriously started to lose their voices. Light-hearted drama, with Patricia Routledge, Dominic Monaghan and Derek Benfield(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Mayor Makepeace hires Hetty to track down his runaway daughter, revealing that she has absconded with her boyfriend and could be in grave danger since both suffer from learning disabilities. Drama, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield(n)

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Gambling Man
programme.desc: Robson Green stars in the title role as Rory Connor, a rent collector who spends his nights at the gaming tables, secure in the knowledge that nobody can turn a card quite like him. Unfortunately, as the stakes get higher, Connor finds himself gambling more than money - with dramatic consequences for himself and those around him. Feature-length drama, with Bernard Hill and Sylvestra Le Touzel(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Cynthia is concerned during a visit as she feels new mum Pamela is overly anxious, so Dr Turner arranges for a psychiatrist to come in. However, they soon discover both mother and baby have gone missing. Meanwhile, Jenny, on secondment, begins to see cracks in the establishment, Chummy discovers her mother has walked out on her father, and Trixie struggles to hide her disappointment when Tom cancels a date. Guest starring Sophie Rundle (The Bletchley Circle)(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Jenny is assigned to first-time mother Jeanette, who requires frequent visits during a protracted labour, and when she meets her patient's cousin, Philip, a new friendship begins to grow for the midwife. Meanwhile, Chummy worries about her mother's illness when she discovers Lady Browne has discharged herself from hospital. Jessica Raine and Miranda Hart star(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reopens the investigation into an actress's disappearance when clips of her being assaulted in a 1990 movie are posted on the internet. Director Don Maddox features in the footage, but when questioned, he appears to have problems distinguishing fact from fiction. A potential witness is then found dead, and Brian and Jack are seconded to the unit handling the case, only to find Emily Driscoll (Dennis Waterman's daughter Hannah) is heading the operation. Imogen Stubbs and Sandra Dickinson also guest star(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki and Jack attend the scene of a fatal car crash in which a driver lost control of her vehicle and the forensic evidence suggests it may not have been an accident and that the victim's son Kevin McDowd - a resident of Kingscote Bows care home - could be involved in the death. The teams begin to notice a pattern of foul play and a connection between the care home and other suspicious deaths that had previously gone unnoticed, while Kevin absconds and takes his friend Serena with him(n)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Part one of two. The team discovers a DNA match between a medical student reported missing six years previously and a corpse that was found at around the same time. Investigations into the man's background reveal he had been suspected of having links to terrorist organisations - but Boyd believes a high-level cover-up is obscuring the whole truth about the case. Guest starring Don Warrington(n)

programme.title: Mansfield Park
programme.desc: Part three of six. Maria gets engaged to Mr Rushworth but is still in love with Henry, while Edmund remains infatuated with Mary - much to Fanny's annoyance(n)

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Period medical drama chronicling the personal and professional consequences of Eleanor Bramwell's determined quest to become the first female surgeon at the East London Hospital. Jemma Redgrave stars(n)

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: Joe receives some bad news from Collette, and his actions end up getting him into trouble. Meanwhile, an unexpected guest opens old wounds for Sam(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: As June prepares for her final day at Sun Hill, Jim finally comes clean and reveals the full extent of his gambling debts - but far from saving his marriage, the admission causes her to storm off in disgust, swearing to have nothing more to do with him. And just when Jim thinks things couldn't possibly get any worse, the hapless copper is abducted and roughed up by vicious loan shark Zaccadelli. Elsewhere, Yvonne discovers her sister has been attacked at home and suspects Shirley is responsible, while Steve is cock-a-hoop after his night with Amber - but she soon brings him down to earth(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Connie is determined to keep everything running smoothly when a health minister visits the hospital, but Lisa sees an opportunity to drum up support for her mentoring scheme. While treating an elderly couple, Jess comes to the realisation that she doesn't love Sean and breaks up with him, turning to her father for emotional support. The tension between Mickie and Mubbs finally comes to a head when she tells him she's gay. Later, an unexpected encounter at the official reception gives him a chance to drown his sorrows(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jayne prepares to unveil her mural overlooking reception, but suffers an accident on the way to the event. Josh learns the official cause of the blaze that claimed his family, while Jude is found by colleagues in a pool of blood(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Gabriel learns Smithy reported him over Kerry's rape allegation and the two come to blows - but retribution comes from an unexpected source when the corrupt copper is gunned down by the Sun Hill sniper. Elsewhere, suspicion falls on Shirley after Yvonne's niece accidentally swallows an Ecstasy tablet, Ramani fears Terry is letting his personal preoccupations interfere with his police work, and a shamefaced Jim tells his colleagues that June has left him(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy returns but Ian is reluctant to talk. Lloyd sees the advantages of his new future. Grant appears edgy and Phil is shocked when he reveals what he is up to(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant tests Phil's family loyalty. Mrs Hewitt has some explanations for Arthur, and Pete is concerned she has made herself at home at the Fowlers'(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: As preparations are made in court for the Maddox tribunal, Hallam confronts the young firefighter himself. Meanwhile, George realises he has blown it with his girlfriend, Julia(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The dealer falls under the suspicion of an ambitious and ruthless police officer, who suspects he is handling stolen European furniture. Meanwhile, Tinker joins Eric for a brief holiday in Essex, where they soon become involved in a smuggling racket. Warren Mitchell and Reece Dinsdale guest star, with Ian McShane and Dudley Sutton(n)

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: Jim is assigned to guard a mainland MP who has come to Jersey to study the island's police force - but nothing prepares him for the torrid time ahead. Drama, starring John Nettles(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Joey promises Billy the use of his Jaguar, and Jack and Adrian run into trouble with some local thugs when a deal goes wrong(n)

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Captain Peacock becomes infatuated with another woman and the staff rally round to conceal the fact from his wife. However, his willingness to go to extreme measures to solve his problem takes Mr Humphries' breath away. Comedy, starring Frank Thornton and John Inman(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean tries to organise a new secretary for Lionel, who is troubled by discussions regarding the mini-series. Rocky's honeymoon with Madge ends in hospital after an unfortunate canoeing accident. Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer star(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: What started as a joke takes on a life of its own when Clegg tells Howard that he was adopted and that his real parents are dead. Alvin is very insistent that he will get a kite to fly - and finds several ways to try to get it done. Long-running comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Robert Fyfe and Brian Murphy(n)

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: The unlikely crime-fighting duo investigate the murder of a Sikh woman whose death has unleashed simmering racial tensions in the town, but as corruption and political intrigue erupt around them, they find that love can thrive against all the odds(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the crime drama, starring Amanda Redman as Superintendent Sandra Pullman, a rising star of the Met who is sidelined after a failed hostage rescue. Put in charge of a new department, she is dismayed to discover the unit is to be manned by a motley crew of old-school former detectives who know little about modern police procedure. However, when they re-examine the case of a man whose conviction for murder 20 years ago has just been overturned, their combined experience and unorthodox methods bring interesting results. Alun Armstrong, Dennis Waterman and James Bolam play Redman's veteran team, with Jon Finch, Tim Woodwork and Philip Bretherton(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The Glaswegian detective is drawn into a suicide case where all is not as it seems - despite his usual attempts to stay out of such investigations. Drama, with Mark McManus, James MacPherson and Blythe Duff, with early appearances from Jason Isaacs (Star Trek Discovery), Deepak Verma (EastEnders) and Meera Syal (Paddington 2). It was directed by Gordon Flemyng, who helmed two Doctor Who big screen offerings, and classic episodes of The Avengers, The Saint and Minder(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The volunteers are short on time for cooking and exercise, so Tom offers solutions to keep them on track, including quick and delicious recipes, and tips for planning ahead. For the first recipe, Tom makes a pot of beef mince - the starting point for some of the nation's favourite dishes. He uses the batch to make a cottage pie, while the rest is frozen to be used later, perhaps for chilli con carne, spaghetti bolognese or lasagne(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Brentford v Leicester City (Kick-off 12.45pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the fourth-round contest, which takes place at Griffin Park, as the Championship Bees take on Brendan Rodgers' Foxes. The home side reached the fifth round last season, before being beaten 4-1 by second-tier rivals Swansea City at the Liberty Stadium. A goal from Emiliano Marcondes was enough to defeat Stoke City here in the third round earlier this month. Leicester suffered the ignominy of losing to League Two's Newport County in round three last year, and Rodgers will be keen to avoid another upset in this game. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Dion Dublin(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Action from day six of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year, which takes place at Melbourne Park, and featured the latest third-round matches. Eventual men's singles champion Novak Djokovic made it through to the last 16 with a four-set victory over Denis Shapovalov at this stage last year, while Japan's Naomi Osaka defeated Hsieh Su-wei 5-7, 6-4, 6-1 en route to her maiden Australian Open title(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max investigate the disappearance of a supermodel, who apparently vanished into thin air when the train she was travelling on went through a tunnel. Elen Rhys stars(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: The dance talent show continues with the fourth and final week of auditions, as the Dance Captains fight to take their team of three acts through to the first live show. programme.title: Pointless Celebrities

programme.title: First & Last

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Marty is betrayed by a man he thought he had fallen in love with, and an injured pilot who had been tasered for attempting to hijack a plane is been brought into the emergency department for treatment. Meanwhile, Archie and Connie bond over their work, and Charlie questions his decision to put Duffy in a care home(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the fourth-round fixtures, including Hull City v Chelsea at the KCOM Stadium and West Ham United v West Bromwich Albion at London Stadium. The Tigers had a dreadful record against the Blues, having lost their last seven meetings in all competitions, including a 4-0 thumping at Stamford Bridge in the fifth round of the 2017/18 FA Cup. Plus, action from games including Brentford v Leicester City at Griffin Park, Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor, Millwall v Sheffield United at the Den, Northampton Town v Derby County at the PTS Academy Stadium, and Queens Park Rangers v Sheffield Wednesday at the Kiyan Prince Foundation Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell present action from the Conference Championship games - Kansas City Chiefs v Tennessee Titans and San Francisco 49ers v Green Bay Packers(n)

programme.title: The Sentinel (2006)
programme.desc: A secret service agent is convinced a member of a neo-Nazi militant group has infiltrated the White House. But his attempt to investigate comes to a halt when he is framed for murder and blackmailed over his affair with the president's wife. As he battles to clear his name, his former protege is given the task of bringing him to justice. Spy thriller, starring Michael Douglas, Kiefer Sutherland, Eva Longoria and Kim Basinger(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the fourth-round fixtures, including Hull City v Chelsea at the KCOM Stadium and West Ham United v West Bromwich Albion at London Stadium. The Tigers had a dreadful record against the Blues, having lost their last seven meetings in all competitions, including a 4-0 thumping at Stamford Bridge in the fifth round of the 2017/18 FA Cup. Plus, action from games including Brentford v Leicester City at Griffin Park, Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor, Millwall v Sheffield United at the Den, Northampton Town v Derby County at the PTS Academy Stadium, and Queens Park Rangers v Sheffield Wednesday at the Kiyan Prince Foundation Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Camouflaged spy cameras go 'undercover' in the animal world. For the first time, cameras follow a family of gorillas, with Spy Grub and Spy Fruit testing their intelligence. Elsewhere in Brazil, Spy Jaguar Cub explores a beautiful gathering of water birds, capybara and caiman, and joins Spy Caiman to film a feeding frenzy(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp takes the bargain hunting teams to Leominster, Herefordshire, where two teams compete to buy three items that will hopefully make the greater profit at auction. Providing a helping hand to the sides are experts Paul Laidlaw and Mark Ashley. Natasha also meets Royal Worcester aficionado Henry Sandon(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester City v Fulham (Kick-off 1.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the fourth-round tie at Etihad Stadium. Championship outfit Fulham have already eliminated one Premier League team when they overcame Aston Villa 2-1 at home. However, they face a far tougher task away to the holders, who beat Port Vale 4-1 in the previous round and comfortably defeated the Cottagers here in both the Premier League and Carabao Cup last season. WIth analysis from Micah Richards and Tim Cahill(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Seventy-five years after the liberation of Auschwitz, Aled Jones visits the National Holocaust Centre in Nottingham, while Pam Rhodes meets survivors, including some who were rescued by the Kindertransport. Plus, hymns from around the country including Abide with Me from Glasgow Cathedral, The Lord's My Shepherd from Tideswell in Derbyshire and Make Me a Channel Of Your Peace from Northampton(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to the Welsh Borders to help a couple with a budget of £775,000 find a countryside home to raise their young son and establish a glamping retreat business(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Shrewsbury Town v Liverpool (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the fourth-round tie at Montgomery Waters Meadow. Defender Aaron Pierre's late strike in a replay against Championship side Bristol City earned the Shrews this lucrative clash. While the likelihood of Jurgen Klopp rotating a squad that is also chasing glory in the league and Europe may give the League One outfit some hope, the Reds were still able to overcome rivals Everton 1-0 in the previous round after making nine changes thanks to Curtis Jones' spectacular winner. With analysis from Ian Wright and Alan Shearer(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Trigger-happy space police the Judoon are targeting 21st-century Gloucester, so the Doctor, Yaz, Ryan and Graham race back to Earth in order to prevent them doing too much damage to the cathedral city. But who are the Judoon looking for and what did they do to incur their wrath? Guest starring Neil Stuke, Jo Martin and Ritu Arya(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne volunteers to put up four trainee doctors at Nonnatus House while they undertake the practical aspect of their obstetrics diploma, hoping the exercise will remind the council how vital the home is to the community and help remove the threat of demolition. There is a lot of excitement about the new arrivals and the doctors don't disappoint. On his CDC rounds, Fred comes across a street marked for demolition, with one house clearly showing signs of life. Period drama, starring Jenny Agutter(n)

programme.title: The Trial of Christine Keeler
programme.desc: Stephen's life hangs in the balance as his sentence is decided at the Old Bailey, while Christine's lie about Lucky Gordon haunts her and she faces trial for perjury. As Profumo rebuilds his reputation by working for an East London charity, and Mandy tries to make a career as a singer, Christine deals with the consequence of being labelled a liar and a prostitute, and reflects on happier days with Stephen and Mandy. Fact-based drama, starring Sophie Cookson, Ben Miles, James Norton and Ellie Bamber(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from today's fourth-round ties, including Manchester City v Fulham at Etihad Stadium and Shrewsbury v Liverpool at Montgomery Waters Meadow. The Citizens were continuing the defence of the trophy they lifted last season, while the Reds were expected to remain in contention to win the cup for the first time since 2006. With analysis from Danny Murphy and Martin Keown(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic hockey gold medallist Sam Quek, Great Britain 400m great Roger Black, Welsh rugby union star Gareth Anscombe and world triathlon champion Jonny Brownlee join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: It has long been believed that eating fat is unhealthy. But is it really as bad as people think? With recent headlines casting doubts on such fears, medic Saleyha Ahsan aims to cut through the confusion. She reveals startling new research that suggests some saturated fats might actually be good for people's health, invites volunteers to find out what happens to their bodies and minds if they stop eating it entirely, and discovers the fat hidden in seawater that could make the food of the future a lot healthier(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crime - Are We Tough Enough?
programme.desc: Barrister Chris Daw and solicitor Ayesha Nayyar look at how Britain is handling law and order, beginning by examining the role of the courts. Chris argues that fewer people need to be in the system, while Ayesha thinks more people should be prosecuted and those found guilty should be sent to prison for longer(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Wallasey on Merseyside, Porth in South Wales, and Ripley in Derbyshire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Cassie Thorn is convinced moving to Australia will be perfect for her family, but her husband Aaron is not so sure, so they and their children sample life in Brisbane for a week(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A man is caught unawares by muggers on the way home after a night out, but his assailants get a surprise of their own, while a launderette owner is targeted by thieves(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell help teams search for items at the Peterborough Festival of Antiques, while Eric Knowles views a horseshoe collection at Oakham Castle, Rutland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel becomes the new resident doctor inside the prison where Jimmi is being held, while Bear is behaving mysteriously and an old friend of Al's claims to have seen a ghost(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A man suspected of killing a police officer is seen near Kembleford and Mallory is determined to catch him, but he has to ask the sleuthing priest for help when disaster strikes(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Dorset to help a couple with £475,000 find a rural property that fits the bill when it comes to a workshop and providing space to keep hens. Jules also delves in the county's glove-making heritage and produces his own bespoke pair(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Farming friends Mark and Sean launch their Herefordshire-inspired crisps at the Droitwich Food Festival, while horticultural pioneer Sophie sells her Juneberry gin(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper scour Northern Ireland for items to sell at auction in Belfast, along the way visiting one of the world's oldest distilleries and sampling seaweed(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Inside Out London
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher discovers why sexual harassment is the norm for women on a night out, while Luke Hanrahan meets the families struggling to survive in modern-day slums. Plus, the programme hears the stories of some of the Holocaust's last-remaining survivors(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Leo continues to cause trouble on the market, but he soon regrets abusing Kat when she hits back at him and attacks his stall. Linda is upset when Mick does not stand up for her against gossips, the Panesars' mother Suki arrives in Walford and makes an immediate impression on Jean as she orders her sons about and Ian revels in his councillor win(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of miles of motorways are being made smart by turning the hard shoulder into a live lane, but many experts think they are dangerous for cars that have broken down in them. Richard Bilton speaks to the families of crash victims and asks whether Britain's motorways are becoming death traps(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Teenagers messing around in a stolen car collide with a pillar in a multistorey car park. The joyriders escape, but within the smashed concrete, the unmistakable form of a skull is revealed. After painstakingly extracting the skeleton encased inside, Nikki and the Lyell team must investigate a suspicious death from 20 years previously. Meanwhile, Clarissa faces a difficult decision regarding her mother's care. First of a two-part episode of the crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, David Caves, Richard Lintern and Liz Carr(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam is back at his parents' house in Leeds intending to prepare for a performance at a local spoken-word night, but instead procrastinates and plays old video games. But when he turns on his childhood computer and stumbles on an old home-made hip-hop track he created with his teenage mates, it triggers a memory(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Outspoken funnyman Frankie Boyle hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour before handing over to the acerbic Simon Evans and actress/stand-up Aisling Bea(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Robert Downey Jr and Emma Thompson, stars of the big-screen fantasy adventure Dolittle, while Hugh Laurie discusses his role in The Personal History of David Copperfield. Monty Python's Terry Gilliam chats about his film The Man Who Killed Don Quixote, while American singer-songwriter Sara Bareilles performs She Used to Be Mine, from her musical Waitress. Plus, another bunch of intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood show more clips from the natural world, including an alien-like snail with pulsating antennae and a 10-metre long animal called a pyrosome(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Chantry Primary take on Chobham Primary in the grand final of the inter-school quiz(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall travels to Indonesia in search of three reptiles to add to his list of predators, including the Komodo dragon and the potentially lethal green pit viper(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Holby City stars Rosie Marcel and Bob Barret look for bargains in the Cambridgeshire and Bedfordshire flatlands, with the expert advice of David Harper and Louise Gostelow. However, the road trip is jeopardised when a flooded river blocks the way to the antiques shops - but a detour leads Bob to a beloved location, where he learns about landscape gardening(n)

programme.title: Baby Chimp Rescue
programme.desc: New arrivals keep arriving at the chimpanzee orphanage in Liberia, bringing Jimmy and Jenny Desmond and their team to breaking point as they find themselves providing around-the-clock care for 38 rescued babies. After losing one of the chimps to illness, they are more determined than ever to build them a sanctuary in the forest where they can live like wild chimps. To prepare for the move, the orphans are learning to make their own nests and get their first taste of life in the forest(n)

programme.title: The Diary of Anne Frank (1959)
programme.desc: Oscar-winning fact-based drama based on the famous journal of a German Jewish girl forced to go into hiding with her family from Nazis in an Amsterdam attic for two years. Starring Millie Perkins, Shelley Winters and Richard Beymer(n)

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the open singles semi-finals. Stewart Anderson and Simon Skelton were the victorious players at this stage last year, prevailing against Les Gillett and Nick Brett respectively. With commentary from David Corkill and John Price(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Michael Baggott are at Grittleton House in Wiltshire, where items include a valuable camera and a 19th-century tribal fighting club. Paul Martin visits a Victorian school that looks the same today as it did 100 years ago(n)

programme.title: Equator from the Air
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan completes his equatorial journey in Southeast Asia. He reveals evidence that Indonesia's capital, Jakarta, is sinking, and discovers how drones are helping to manage a dangerous conflict between elephants and farmers in Sumatra. In areas of Borneo afflicted by deforestation, he searches for orangutans at night, and helps the veterinarian team health-check orphaned apes at the rehabilitation centre in Sepilok, Malaysia. Finally, he finds out how aerial tech offers hope for endangered Siamese crocodiles(n)

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: A look across Hawaii's varied islands, discovering how they were made, and how its wildlife thrives. The most remote island chain on Earth, its tropical shores are hard to reach. However, for the hardy creatures that have made it there, such as the waterfall-climbing fish, carnivorous caterpillar and Laysan albatross, a land of opportunity awaits. From newly formed lava fields, to lush jungles, and vibrant coral reefs, these diverse and beautiful islands have it all. As this documentary reveals, its people are looking to the future and attempting to protect and foster its flora and fauna(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring and the platoon welcome the first contingent of American troops to Walmington-on-Sea. All goes well initially, but when the visiting soldiers are told to make themselves at home, they show a little too much interest in the Home Guard's girlfriends, putting Anglo-US relations under strain. Classic comedy first aired in 1973, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo returns to explore more of the US rail network, embarking on the first leg of a journey across Alaska. He begins his journey amid the snow-capped mountains of the Kenal Peninsula, where he discovers that 150 years ago, Alaska was a Russian colony. Moving on, in Seward he feeds a rescued sea otter with a fearsome bite and in Anchorage he joins the US Arctic Warriors for parachute training(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on animals which start with a 'q', this time inviting Daliso Chaponda, Cariad Lloyd and Phill Jupitus to join regular panellist Alan Davies. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Untouchable: The Rise and Fall of Harvey Weinstein
programme.desc: The story of how the film producer acquired and deployed his power, charting how he allegedly abused women with seeming impunity throughout his career - from his early days as a music promoter to running movie company Miramax with his brother Bob. Women who came forward to accuse Weinstein of sexual harassment and assault tell their stories in detail, including Rosanna Arquette, Erika Rosenbaum and Paz De La Huerta, while the reporters who broke the story detail his ruthless attempts to preserve power, even as scandal threatened to engulf him(n)

programme.title: Z for Zachariah (2015)
programme.desc: A woman lives alone on a farm that belonged to her family in the aftermath of a nuclear apocalypse, believing herself to be the last survivor of the human race. Her simple life is disrupted by the appearance of a second survivor, and they adapt to working together - but the arrival of a third individual threatens everything they have built. Sci-fi drama based on Robert C O'Brien's novel, starring Margot Robbie, Chiwetel Ejiofor and Chris Pine(n)

programme.title: International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents action from the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the open singles semi-finals. With commentary from David Corkill and John Price(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Shappi Khorsandi and Konnie Huq search the antiques shops of Sussex in the hope of making a profit, with expert guidance from Tim Medhust and Charles Hanson. Charles is determined to impress Shappi but a fishy buy could see them flounder at the north London auction, while Konnie is put through her paces with some tough haggling for an Arts and Crafts oak armchair(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Return of the programme in which presenter Helen Skelton and designer Danny Clarke transform unloved gardens in a single day, beginning with a back garden suffering from the after effects of flooding in York(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team visits Ballycastle in Co Antrim, a coastal community that is becoming known for its crafts and artisan food and drink. Margherita Taylor heads to a farm that produces ethical and sustainable produce, Joe Crowley catches up with a couple who make award-winning smoked salmon and dulse seaweed, and Charlotte Smith goes on a red squirrel safari with a school group(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The host explores the scenery of the west coast of the United States, beginning in Arizona where he learns about cacti and marvels at the sight of them in the desert and in private gardens where they have replaced traditional plants and lawns. In Palm Springs, he views gardens with famous owners, from film stars to big businessmen and politicians. Los Angeles is Monty's next stop where he hears how schoolchildren are taking up gardening. His trip ends in Seattle where he visits a tropical getaway for one of the city's main workforces and views some beautiful green spaces just outside the city(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The contestants are given just three hours to produce their ultimate children's birthday cake - and as if that were not tough enough, Mary Berry reminds them she knows a thing or two about baking. This is followed by Chris Bavin's challenge to rustle something up from leftovers, and he makes the task twice as hard by stripping back the contents of the larders. The judges then decide who will have to compete in Angela Hartnett's eliminator, by the end of which another contestant will be hanging up their apron. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley tests whether diet can affect mental health, and Alain Gregoire investigates the future technology that could spot signs of dementia before it develops. Elsewhere, Zoe Williams reveals a new treatment for the skin condition psoriasis, and Guddi Singh finds out the best way to calm a crying baby(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Katherine Downes presents action from day seven of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year, which takes place at Melbourne Park, and featured fourth-round matches. Six-time champion Roger Federer was defeated by rising star Stefanos Tsitsipas at this stage last year(n)

programme.title: Live International Bowls
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents coverage of the World Indoor Championships from Potters Resort in Hopton-on-Sea in Norfolk, featuring the open singles final. Stewart Anderson defeated Simon Skelton in last year's showpiece match to win the title for the second time in his career. With commentary from David Corkill and John Price(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Revisiting historic venues from the series, alongside new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin visits the Cotswolds village of Broadway to uncover its artistic past(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Reports on rural developments from the Peak District. Anita Rani finds out about a campaign to save Bamford's village pub and joins a group of ramblers on a trip into the surrounding countryside. Sean Fletcher meets a grocer who pulls together all the best produce from local farmers, while Helen Skelton goes rock climbing. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes some new arrivals on the farm and Tom Heap reports on the financial impact of environmentally friendly farming(n)

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: Action from the World Cup meeting in Kitzbuhel, where the celebrated Hahnenkamm took place. Plus, an interview with snowboarder Max Parrot, who returned to the sport after beating cancer to take X-Games gold(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuiness, Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff go on a road trip in a trio of affordable second-hand convertibles. On the test track, Chris puts the new Ariel Atom through its paces, while Freddie goes bungee jumping off a dam in an old Rover(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: Six couples are challenged to prove their survival skills in Alaska's harsh wilderness, with the most successful pair winning a remarkable home miles from the nearest road, which was built from scratch by its original owners. In the first episode, they receive a crash course in what to do when encountering a bear before being sent into the woods to gather material and build shelters. They must then fell trees, make a fire and brave the freezing waters of Lost Lake(n)

programme.title: Keeler, Profumo, Ward and Me
programme.desc: Tom Mangold shares his experiences of reporting first-hand on the Profumo scandal, and shares the interviews he has conducted with several of the key players, including an exclusive audiotape interview with Stephen Ward, recorded two weeks before his suicide in 1963. Mangold shares his memories of Ward, whom he believes was unfairly scapegoated, and presents extracts from interviews conducted with both Christine Keeler and Mandy Rice-Davies, which have never been broadcast(n)

programme.title: The Boy in the Striped Pyjamas (2008)
programme.desc: Bruno, a boy in Nazi Germany, moves to the country when his father is placed in charge of a concentration camp. While exploring the woods close to the facility's boundary, he spots a Jewish youngster being held there, and the two become friends - but Bruno remains innocently oblivious to the camp's true purpose. Drama, starring David Thewlis, Asa Butterfield and Jack Scanlon(n)

programme.title: The Fits (2015)
programme.desc: An 11-year-old tomboy becomes entranced with a tight-knit dance team in Cincinnati's West End. Enamoured by the power and confidence of this strong community of girls, she eagerly absorbs routines, masters drills and even pierces her own ears to fit in. A mysterious outbreak of fainting spells plagues the team, and Toni finds herself caught up in danger as her desire for acceptance is twisted. Teen drama, starring Royalty Hightower and Alexis Neblett(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Stoke Newington, Hackney, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: A family home with an industrial twist, a sleek, split-level semi-detached property, and an eclectic Edwardian abode with a menagerie of surprises are all judged by neighbours(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Serena needs to avoid causing trouble with an inspection looming, but when Jason has problems with the privatised porter company, she must decide if she can afford to help him. Nicky finds herself having to treat her own mother, who has been involved in a road accident, and is forced to take desperate measures to help her. Essie realises that she has to tell Ben everything about the illegal adoption if they are to stay together, but is not sure she can trust him with the truth(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth browse the emporiums of north-east England, where Angus buys an expensive pen and Steven plumps for a loving jug. They then head to the deciding auction in Dundee(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Making a living in farming today is hard work and taking big risks can help a farm survive. Here, three inspirational farming families are celebrated for their ideas and ingenuity, but only one can be crowned Diversification Farmer of the Year at the Farmers Weekly Awards held in London. Narrated by Denis Lawson(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: In the last programme of knockout week, the second group of five face two tough tests. First they are given three hours, with limited equipment and facilities, to produce a knockout plate that showcases their versatility and style. This dish is judged by a group of diners specialising in the street food and pop-up industry - and the contestant that wins the most votes sails through to the semi-finals. The other four are left to produce a showstopper dish in just 90 minutes - hoping it will be enough to get them through(n)

programme.title: This Week in Parliament
programme.desc: Highlights of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day at Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Roger and Margaret want to leave the winter in Liverpool behind and rent a property in southern Spain, with local agent Toni on hand to help them. Gloria Hunniford narrates(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of dishes using game. The menu includes a venison dish from Rachel Khoo, Keith Floyd cooking with pheasant recipe, Paul Hollywood and Mary Berry's raised game pie, Raymond Blanc cooking with pigeon, and a rabbit dish from Nigel Slater(n)

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: A double bill of challenges as Eric Knowles faces Ochuko Ojiri at an auction in Colchester, before Kate Bliss and Caroline Hawley square up at an antiques fair in Surrey(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Katherine Downes presents action from day eight of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year, which takes place at Melbourne Park, and featured fourth-round matches(n)

programme.title: Indian Ocean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Adventurer Simon Reeve sets out to travel around the coastline of the Indian Ocean, journeying by boat, car, train and plane as he takes in 16 countries. He starts on the tip of South Africa, where he dives with ragged-tooth sharks and joins conservationists helping to save the endangered African penguin, before heading into Cape Town, where he has a close encounter with a drug dealer. Moving on, he hitches a boat to Mozambique, where a nervous captain reveals the threat posed by Somali pirates, and completes this leg of his journey in Zanzibar, a tropical island steeped in the long history of the slave trade(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Ushaw in Co Durham, antique experts David Fletcher and James Lewis put a value on the region's antiques and collectibles. David is delighted by a 1950s toy plane, while James finds a 1940s menu from a famous Hollywood restaurant which has been signed by some top film stars. Host Paul Martin visits the Mining Art Gallery in Bishop Auckland to find out why many coal miners were inspired to pick up a paintbrush(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radzi Chinyanganya, Hal Cruttenden, Kerry Godliman and Ebony Rainford-Brent go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a new quest as he travels around South-east Asia, guided by his 1913 Bradshaw's Handbook on a 2,500-mile railway adventure across six countries. Beginning in Hong Kong, the former politician investigates how Britain won the island and Kowloon from China after two mid-19th century wars over the trade in opium, before boarding the island's most famous funicular to the Peak, and straddling a bamboo pole to learn the traditional Cantonese art of noodle-making(n)

programme.title: Holocaust Memorial Day
programme.desc: Seventy five years after the liberation of Auschwitz concentration camp, more than 150 survivors attend a commemoration to mark Holocaust Memorial Day. Through music, poetry and powerful personal testimony, all those who were persecuted by the Nazis, as well as those who were victims of later genocides are remembered. Among those taking part are cellist Sheku Kanneh-Mason accompanied by his brother Braimah, actor Simon Russell Beale, actresses Rebecca Front, Nina Wadia and Georgina Campbell, and the Fourth Choir. Huw Edwards presents(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another second-round match as the Endeavours take on the Junipers and with both teams having won one game and lost another, this is their last chance to stay in the tournament. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the first of the quarter-final matches, two teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches to progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: The Windermere Children
programme.desc: In August 1945, psychologist Oscar Friedmann and his team of counsellors look after child survivors of the Holocaust who were sent to the Lake District to begin new lives. Friedmann and art therapist Marie Paneth, philanthropist Leonard Montefiore and sports coach Jock Lawrence have four months to help the children reclaim their lives, while waiting for news of their loved ones. Fact-based drama, starring Thomas Kretschmann, Romola Garai, Tim McInnerny and Iain Glen(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Wild Scotland
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan takes a trip home to the island of Mull where he has just one day to film the rare sea eagle. While searching for a nest site, he spots a host of other wildlife(n)

programme.title: The Last Survivors
programme.desc: Documentary compiling the personal testimonies of the remaining Holocaust survivors living in Britain, all of whom were children during their incarceration in the concentration camps. They discuss how these experiences continue to influence their adult lives and why particular memories have stayed with them. The film follows one man on a return journey to Auschwitz, accompanied by his daughter, while another survivor revisits his home town in Germany for the first time since 1946(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: After being colonised by humans, Puerto Rico's rich natural resources were rapidly exploited for agriculture, industry and urban development. This documentary follows a conservation team fighting to save the most endangered creatures from extinction after they have been pushed to the edge of existence by human encroachment. David Attenborough narrates(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Tessa Dunlop hitches a ride with the Thames river police to the new London Gateway, where she gets an overview of Britain's trade with the world from the vantage point of a giant crane. From Ramsgate, Kent, she embarks on one of the Dunkirk 'little ships' to discover the vital role it played during the Second World War, before making her way to Dover where she finds a magnificent Roman lighthouse that has guarded the shores for centuries. Plus, Neil Oliver reveals the stories of trade and defence that characterise England's south east coast(n)

programme.title: Wisting
programme.desc: With the investigator still breathing down his neck, William is determined to clear his name, while Line helps him to chase leads that his colleagues cannot follow up. Hammer and Benjamin encounter a suspect from a past case while helping the search for Linne. Norwegian crime drama, starring Sven Nordin(n)

programme.title: Wisting
programme.desc: The killer is still out there, but William is trapped in prison after being arrested for obstruction of justice and needs to get out or else Line will be investigating the case alone. Norwegian crime drama, starring Sven Nordin(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Los Angeles
programme.desc: The Australian explores the notion of success in Los Angeles and talks to various experts and advisors as well as to the stars themselves(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Steve Wright and Simon Mayo present the edition first shown on February 2nd. Featuring performances by Holly Johnson, Roy Orbison, Robert Howard and Kym Mazelle, Sheena Easton, Michael Ball, Yazz, Hue and Cry, Samantha Fox, Simply Red, Roachford, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, and Bobby Brown(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mike Read and Sybil Ruscoe present the edition first shown on February 9th. Featuring performances by Samantha Fox, Rick Astley, Yazz, Hue and Cry, Def Leppard, Poison, Texas, Michael Ball, Bobby Brown, Morrissey, Marc Almond and Gene Pitney, and Mike and the Mechanics(n)

programme.title: Robert Burns - The People's Poet
programme.desc: Andrew O'Hagan celebrates the life of Scotland's most famous poet, and explores the reasons why Burns remains such a cultural force. The author examines the politics, patriotism and sense of place of the man known in his native land as the Bard(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Tessa Dunlop hitches a ride with the Thames river police to the new London Gateway, where she gets an overview of Britain's trade with the world from the vantage point of a giant crane. From Ramsgate, Kent, she embarks on one of the Dunkirk 'little ships' to discover the vital role it played during the Second World War, before making her way to Dover where she finds a magnificent Roman lighthouse that has guarded the shores for centuries. Plus, Neil Oliver reveals the stories of trade and defence that characterise England's south east coast(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull head to Northern Ireland, where they find out about the invention of the ejector seat, and visit Armagh Observatory, a railway museum located inside a barber shop, and SS Nomadic, known as Titanic's little sister(n)

programme.title: Addicted to Sheep
programme.desc: A year in the life of a family of North Pennines tenant hill farmers. As Tom and Kay Hutchinson work through sun, rain, sleet and snow to try to breed the perfect sheep, their three children attend the local primary school, which is comprised entirely of farmers' children(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following the daily lives of a bottlenose dolphin as it struggles to keep its newborn calf alive in the treacherous waters of Shark Bay, Western Australia. With the help of other females, the creature tries to teach the vulnerable baby the necessary tricks for survival in shark-infested waters(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The focus turns to Britain's public art collections, in which 17,000 paintings are listed as 'artist unknown'. From these Philip Mould and his research director Bendor Grosvenor identify several works they believe are by Thomas Gainsborough. They choose two paintings, the first being an 18th-century portrait of St Albans mayor Joseph Gape that Bendor thinks dates back to the artist's days as a high-society portrait painter - although he'll need to convince the world's leading Gainsborough expert. Philip's choice is a rare landscape, painted when the artist was experimenting with dreamlike scenes. But why is conservationist Aviva Burnstock troubled by a distinctive blue pigment?(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from New York
programme.desc: The Australian journalist reluctantly revisits the Big Apple and interviews famous New Yorkers including model Ivana Trump. He also visits a health spa and hires a bodyguard(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Following the creation of the British composer's latest opera, Lessons in Love and Violence, which premiered at the Royal Opera House this year. The film follows Benjamin's journey from composing songs at the age of just three to being transfixed by Fantasia and 2001: Space Odyssey, and his days as the youngest pupil ever of Messiaen in Paris(n)

programme.title: Bunkers, Brutalism and Bloodymindedness: Concrete Poetry with Jonathan Meades
programme.desc: Jonathan Meades concludes a two-part documentary celebrating 20th-century Brutalist architecture, which typically takes the form of block-like concrete constructions. The presenter explores the post-war work of architect Le Corbusier, and argues that buildings once maligned by critics stood for optimism and grandeur(n)

programme.title: Architects of the Divine: The First Gothic Age
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the development of the Perpendicular style, which took Britain away from the French-inspired buildings of the medieval age. The buildings examined include Gloucester Cathedral, which was built to commemorate a murdered monarch, and King's College Chapel in Cambridge, one of the last buildings to be constructed in the style. Presented by Nina Ramirez(n)

programme.title: Addicted to Sheep
programme.desc: A year in the life of a family of North Pennines tenant hill farmers. As Tom and Kay Hutchinson work through sun, rain, sleet and snow to try to breed the perfect sheep, their three children attend the local primary school, which is comprised entirely of farmers' children(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: The crew heads down the Croatian coast, where Francesco da Mosto's first challenge is to scale the heights of the main mast to hang the Venetian flag aloft. They drop anchor in the beautiful city of Split where they visit Roman Emperor Diocletian's retirement villa - the oldest lived-in palace in the world(n)

programme.title: A Very British Romance with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian delves into 300 years of love's rituals to uncover the forces shaping the happy ever after, revealing how people's most intimate thoughts and feelings have been affected by social, political and cultural ideas. Lucy begins in the Georgian age, when - after centuries of marriage being as much about business as love - women began to have an unprecedented degree of romantic freedom(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: An exploration of the far-reaching implications of a genome editing technology CRISPR, which opens the door to curing diseases, reshaping the biosphere and allowing people to design their own children. The documentary looks at the revolutionary method through the eyes of the scientists who discovered it, the families it is affecting and the bio-engineers who are testing its limits(n)

programme.title: The Windermere Children: In Their Own Words
programme.desc: The story of the pioneering project to rehabilitate child survivors of the Holocaust on the shores of Lake Windermere, with first-hand testimonies from some of those who attended. Reflecting on the survivors' lives after Windermere, the film includes home-movie footage and footage of their annual reunions(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough has a passion for birds' eggs, which protect the young from the outside world at the same time as allowing them to breathe and are strong enough to withstand the full weight of an incubating parent and weak enough to allow a chick to break free. In this documentary, the broadcaster reveals the secrets of birds' eggs from creation to hatching, exploring how they are made, the reasons behind their shape and their intrinsic purpose(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Brentford v Leicester City (Kick-off 12.45pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the fourth-round contest, which takes place at Griffin Park, as the Championship Bees take on Brendan Rodgers' Foxes. The home side reached the fifth round last season, before being beaten 4-1 by second-tier rivals Swansea City at the Liberty Stadium. A goal from Emiliano Marcondes was enough to defeat Stoke City here in the third round earlier this month. Leicester suffered the ignominy of losing to League Two's Newport County in round three last year, and Rodgers will be keen to avoid another upset in this game. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Dion Dublin(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Action from day six of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year, which takes place at Melbourne Park, and featured the latest third-round matches. Eventual men's singles champion Novak Djokovic made it through to the last 16 with a four-set victory over Denis Shapovalov at this stage last year, while Japan's Naomi Osaka defeated Hsieh Su-wei 5-7, 6-4, 6-1 en route to her maiden Australian Open title(n)
