programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: In Pembrokeshire, Kate meets the last master thatcher based in Wales. She also helps with the harvesting of water reeds - an invasive, thick grass-like plant now used on 80 per cent of thatched roofs in the UK. In Pendine Sands, Kate joins a cross-country hunt adapted for modern times and struggles to stay on the horse as they chase hounds who have been specifically trained to only pick up on human scent. Finally, Kate and Teg visit an agricultural college that helps neuro-diverse students thrive through a deep connection with the land(n)

programme.title: The 1900 Island
programme.desc: The four families face their final week on the small Anglesey island, and with one last haul of fish everyone is counting down the days. But as the outside world comes calling, modern mechanisation hits their rural idyll, prices plummet and there is a rude awakening for the men and women who resort to cockling and gathering shellfish to makes ends meet(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction in the spa town of Malvern, Worcestershire. Presented by Eric Knowles(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester City v Fulham (Kick-off 1.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the fourth-round tie at Etihad Stadium. Championship outfit Fulham have already eliminated one Premier League team when they overcame Aston Villa 2-1 at home. However, they face a far tougher task away to the holders, who beat Port Vale 4-1 in the previous round and comfortably defeated the Cottagers here in both the Premier League and Carabao Cup last season. WIth analysis from Micah Richards and Tim Cahill(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Seventy-five years after the liberation of Auschwitz, Aled Jones visits the National Holocaust Centre in Nottingham, while Pam Rhodes meets survivors, including some who were rescued by the Kindertransport. Plus, hymns from around the country including Abide with Me from Glasgow Cathedral, The Lord's My Shepherd from Tideswell in Derbyshire and Make Me a Channel Of Your Peace from Northampton(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to the Welsh Borders to help a couple with a budget of £775,000 find a countryside home to raise their young son and establish a glamping retreat business(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Shrewsbury Town v Liverpool (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the fourth-round tie at Montgomery Waters Meadow. Defender Aaron Pierre's late strike in a replay against Championship side Bristol City earned the Shrews this lucrative clash. While the likelihood of Jurgen Klopp rotating a squad that is also chasing glory in the league and Europe may give the League One outfit some hope, the Reds were still able to overcome rivals Everton 1-0 in the previous round after making nine changes thanks to Curtis Jones' spectacular winner. With analysis from Ian Wright and Alan Shearer(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Trigger-happy space police the Judoon are targeting 21st-century Gloucester, so the Doctor, Yaz, Ryan and Graham race back to Earth in order to prevent them doing too much damage to the cathedral city. But who are the Judoon looking for and what did they do to incur their wrath? Guest starring Neil Stuke, Jo Martin and Ritu Arya(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne volunteers to put up four trainee doctors at Nonnatus House while they undertake the practical aspect of their obstetrics diploma, hoping the exercise will remind the council how vital the home is to the community and help remove the threat of demolition. There is a lot of excitement about the new arrivals and the doctors don't disappoint. On his CDC rounds, Fred comes across a street marked for demolition, with one house clearly showing signs of life. Period drama, starring Jenny Agutter(n)

programme.title: The Trial of Christine Keeler
programme.desc: Stephen's life hangs in the balance as his sentence is decided at the Old Bailey, while Christine's lie about Lucky Gordon haunts her and she faces trial for perjury. As Profumo rebuilds his reputation by working for an East London charity, and Mandy tries to make a career as a singer, Christine deals with the consequence of being labelled a liar and a prostitute, and reflects on happier days with Stephen and Mandy. Fact-based drama, starring Sophie Cookson, Ben Miles, James Norton and Ellie Bamber(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from today's fourth-round ties, including Manchester City v Fulham at Etihad Stadium and Shrewsbury v Liverpool at Montgomery Waters Meadow. The Citizens were continuing the defence of the trophy they lifted last season, while the Reds were expected to remain in contention to win the cup for the first time since 2006. With analysis from Danny Murphy and Martin Keown(n)

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: Return of the programme that goes behind the scenes with Gwent Police's dedicated forensic collision investigators as they deal with serious incidents on the roads. In the opening edition, a hit-and-run leaves a cyclist fighting for her life, while mystery surrounds the death of a delivery driver on a busy dual carriageway(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: It has long been believed that eating fat is unhealthy. But is it really as bad as people think? With recent headlines casting doubts on such fears, medic Saleyha Ahsan aims to cut through the confusion. She reveals startling new research that suggests some saturated fats might actually be good for people's health, invites volunteers to find out what happens to their bodies and minds if they stop eating it entirely, and discovers the fat hidden in seawater that could make the food of the future a lot healthier(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crime - Are We Tough Enough?
programme.desc: Barrister Chris Daw and solicitor Ayesha Nayyar look at how Britain is handling law and order, beginning by examining the role of the courts. Chris argues that fewer people need to be in the system, while Ayesha thinks more people should be prosecuted and those found guilty should be sent to prison for longer(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Wallasey on Merseyside, Porth in South Wales, and Ripley in Derbyshire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Cassie Thorn is convinced moving to Australia will be perfect for her family, but her husband Aaron is not so sure, so they and their children sample life in Brisbane for a week(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway visits two places shaped by the Industrial Revolution. In Powys, he joins fellow weather presenter Sian Lloyd at Lake Vyrnwy, where the pair learn about the village flooded to make way for the reservoir that supplies Liverpool with water. They also marvel at the famous dam, a Victorian engineering feat. In Tredegar, Derek's on a trail back in time, discovering the rich history of the valleys town. He visits the largest lump of coal in the world, learns about a scheme that inspired the creation of the NHS and views the impressive remains of Sirhowy ironwork furnaces(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell help teams search for items at the Peterborough Festival of Antiques, while Eric Knowles views a horseshoe collection at Oakham Castle, Rutland(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel becomes the new resident doctor inside the prison where Jimmi is being held, while Bear is behaving mysteriously and an old friend of Al's claims to have seen a ghost(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A man suspected of killing a police officer is seen near Kembleford and Mallory is determined to catch him, but he has to ask the sleuthing priest for help when disaster strikes(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Dorset to help a couple with £475,000 find a rural property that fits the bill when it comes to a workshop and providing space to keep hens. Jules also delves in the county's glove-making heritage and produces his own bespoke pair(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Farming friends Mark and Sean launch their Herefordshire-inspired crisps at the Droitwich Food Festival, while horticultural pioneer Sophie sells her Juneberry gin(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper scour Northern Ireland for items to sell at auction in Belfast, along the way visiting one of the world's oldest distilleries and sampling seaweed(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: It has been described as one of the biggest scandals in the Welsh NHS since devolutio, with parents and babies being failed for years at two hospitals run by the Cwm Taf Morgannwg University Health Board. In this BBC Wales Investigates programme, Wyre Davies examines why patients were put at risk time and time again - and why so far no one has been held to account(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Leo continues to cause trouble on the market, but he soon regrets abusing Kat when she hits back at him and attacks his stall. Linda is upset when Mick does not stand up for her against gossips, the Panesars' mother Suki arrives in Walford and makes an immediate impression on Jean as she orders her sons about and Ian revels in his councillor win(n)

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: When calls start coming in to report debris on the M4, police discover that a father and son have been in a serious crash. As traffic chaos builds, the pressure is on for the collision team to find out why the car left the road(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Teenagers messing around in a stolen car collide with a pillar in a multistorey car park. The joyriders escape, but within the smashed concrete, the unmistakable form of a skull is revealed. After painstakingly extracting the skeleton encased inside, Nikki and the Lyell team must investigate a suspicious death from 20 years previously. Meanwhile, Clarissa faces a difficult decision regarding her mother's care. First of a two-part episode of the crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, David Caves, Richard Lintern and Liz Carr(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of miles of motorways are being made smart by turning the hard shoulder into a live lane, but many experts think they are dangerous for cars that have broken down in them. Richard Bilton speaks to the families of crash victims and asks whether Britain's motorways are becoming death traps(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam is back at his parents' house in Leeds intending to prepare for a performance at a local spoken-word night, but instead procrastinates and plays old video games. But when he turns on his childhood computer and stumbles on an old home-made hip-hop track he created with his teenage mates, it triggers a memory(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Robert Downey Jr and Emma Thompson, stars of the big-screen fantasy adventure Dolittle, while Hugh Laurie discusses his role in The Personal History of David Copperfield. Monty Python's Terry Gilliam chats about his film The Man Who Killed Don Quixote, while American singer-songwriter Sara Bareilles performs She Used to Be Mine, from her musical Waitress. Plus, another bunch of intrepid audience members willing to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ken Hom and Ching-He Huang, and special guest Fay Ripley faces her food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The volunteers are short on time for cooking and exercise, so Tom offers solutions to keep them on track, including quick and delicious recipes, and tips for planning ahead. For the first recipe, Tom makes a pot of beef mince - the starting point for some of the nation's favourite dishes. He uses the batch to make a cottage pie, while the rest is frozen to be used later, perhaps for chilli con carne, spaghetti bolognese or lasagne(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Brentford v Leicester City (Kick-off 12.45pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the fourth-round contest, which takes place at Griffin Park, as the Championship Bees take on Brendan Rodgers' Foxes. The home side reached the fifth round last season, before being beaten 4-1 by second-tier rivals Swansea City at the Liberty Stadium. A goal from Emiliano Marcondes was enough to defeat Stoke City here in the third round earlier this month. Leicester suffered the ignominy of losing to League Two's Newport County in round three last year, and Rodgers will be keen to avoid another upset in this game. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Dion Dublin(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Action from day six of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year, which takes place at Melbourne Park, and featured the latest third-round matches. Eventual men's singles champion Novak Djokovic made it through to the last 16 with a four-set victory over Denis Shapovalov at this stage last year, while Japan's Naomi Osaka defeated Hsieh Su-wei 5-7, 6-4, 6-1 en route to her maiden Australian Open title(n)

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie is joined by Steven Thompson and Julie Fleeting to present all the results and news from today's matches across the country(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max investigate the disappearance of a supermodel, who apparently vanished into thin air when the train she was travelling on went through a tunnel. Elen Rhys stars(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: The dance talent show continues with the fourth and final week of auditions, as the Dance Captains fight to take their team of three acts through to the first live show. The dancers need to impress at least 75 per cent of the audience for the mirror to open and progress in the competition. Who will make it through to the next round?(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special sports edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Tanni Grey-Thompson, David Wilkie, Matthew Hoggard, Graeme Swann, Jo Pavey, Ade Adepitan, Helen Glover and Heather Stanning(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Marty is betrayed by a man he thought he had fallen in love with, and an injured pilot who had been tasered for attempting to hijack a plane is been brought into the emergency department for treatment. Meanwhile, Archie and Connie bond over their work, and Charlie questions his decision to put Duffy in a care home(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the fourth-round fixtures, including Hull City v Chelsea at the KCOM Stadium and West Ham United v West Bromwich Albion at London Stadium. The Tigers had a dreadful record against the Blues, having lost their last seven meetings in all competitions, including a 4-0 thumping at Stamford Bridge in the fifth round of the 2017/18 FA Cup. Plus, action from games including Brentford v Leicester City at Griffin Park, Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor, Millwall v Sheffield United at the Den, Northampton Town v Derby County at the PTS Academy Stadium, and Queens Park Rangers v Sheffield Wednesday at the Kiyan Prince Foundation Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell present action from the Conference Championship games - Kansas City Chiefs v Tennessee Titans and San Francisco 49ers v Green Bay Packers(n)

programme.title: The Sentinel (2006)
programme.desc: A secret service agent is convinced a member of a neo-Nazi militant group has infiltrated the White House. But his attempt to investigate comes to a halt when he is framed for murder and blackmailed over his affair with the president's wife. As he battles to clear his name, his former protege is given the task of bringing him to justice. Spy thriller, starring Michael Douglas, Kiefer Sutherland, Eva Longoria and Kim Basinger(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the fourth-round fixtures, including Hull City v Chelsea at the KCOM Stadium and West Ham United v West Bromwich Albion at London Stadium. programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Scotland
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, with reports from around Scotland(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Camouflaged spy cameras go 'undercover' in the animal world. For the first time, cameras follow a family of gorillas, with Spy Grub and Spy Fruit testing their intelligence. Elsewhere in Brazil, Spy Jaguar Cub explores a beautiful gathering of water birds, capybara and caiman, and joins Spy Caiman to film a feeding frenzy(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp takes the bargain hunting teams to Leominster, Herefordshire, where two teams compete to buy three items that will hopefully make the greater profit at auction. Providing a helping hand to the sides are experts Paul Laidlaw and Mark Ashley. Natasha also meets Royal Worcester aficionado Henry Sandon(n)

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Westro skipper James West and his have been in the North Sea for three days hunting prawns, but so far have struggled to find a catch so he decides to travel 120 miles in the hope of more luck. New recruit Gavin Burnett heads out on Peterhead trawler Ocean Endeavour, with the 17-year-old being given the responsibilty of bringing the fish on board and cooking for the crew. On the night of the Fishing News Awards, James is nominated for Fisherman of the Year, while rookie Gavin is hoping to land the Trainee of the Year awar(n)

programme.desc: Scottish music legends Alan Allen and Duncan Bridge unexpectedly 'retired' in 1978 at the height of their fame. Here, they mark their return granting BBC Scotland with an access-all-areas film following the recording of their much-anticipated comeback album of duets. Spoof documentary, starring Mike Keat and Archie Easton, with appearances by the Proclaimers, KT Tunstall and Mel C(n)

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Action from day six of the opening Grand Slam tournament of the year, which takes place at Melbourne Park, and featured the latest third-round matches. Eventual men's singles champion Novak Djokovic made it through to the last 16 with a four-set victory over Denis Shapovalov at this stage last year, while Japan's Naomi Osaka defeated Hsieh Su-wei 5-7, 6-4, 6-1 en route to her maiden Australian Open title(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max investigate the disappearance of a supermodel, who apparently vanished into thin air when the train she was travelling on went through a tunnel. Elen Rhys stars(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: The dance talent show continues with the fourth and final week of auditions, as the Dance Captains fight to take their team of three acts through to the first live show. The dancers need to impress at least 75 per cent of the audience for the mirror to open and progress in the competition. Who will make it through to the next round?(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special sports edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Tanni Grey-Thompson, David Wilkie, Matthew Hoggard, Graeme Swann, Jo Pavey, Ade Adepitan, Helen Glover and Heather Stanning(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Marty is betrayed by a man he thought he had fallen in love with, and an injured pilot who had been tasered for attempting to hijack a plane is been brought into the emergency department for treatment. Meanwhile, Archie and Connie bond over their work, and Charlie questions his decision to put Duffy in a care home(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the fourth-round fixtures, including Hull City v Chelsea at the KCOM Stadium and West Ham United v West Bromwich Albion at London Stadium. The Tigers had a dreadful record against the Blues, having lost their last seven meetings in all competitions, including a 4-0 thumping at Stamford Bridge in the fifth round of the 2017/18 FA Cup. Plus, action from games including Brentford v Leicester City at Griffin Park, Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor, Millwall v Sheffield United at the Den, Northampton Town v Derby County at the PTS Academy Stadium, and Queens Park Rangers v Sheffield Wednesday at the Kiyan Prince Foundation Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman, Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell present action from the Conference Championship games - Kansas City Chiefs v Tennessee Titans and San Francisco 49ers v Green Bay Packers(n)

programme.title: The Sentinel (2006)
programme.desc: A secret service agent is convinced a member of a neo-Nazi militant group has infiltrated the White House. But his attempt to investigate comes to a halt when he is framed for murder and blackmailed over his affair with the president's wife. As he battles to clear his name, his former protege is given the task of bringing him to justice. Spy thriller, starring Michael Douglas, Kiefer Sutherland, Eva Longoria and Kim Basinger(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from the fourth-round fixtures, including Hull City v Chelsea at the KCOM Stadium and West Ham United v West Bromwich Albion at London Stadium. The Tigers had a dreadful record against the Blues, having lost their last seven meetings in all competitions, including a 4-0 thumping at Stamford Bridge in the fifth round of the 2017/18 FA Cup. Plus, action from games including Brentford v Leicester City at Griffin Park, Burnley v Norwich City at Turf Moor, Millwall v Sheffield United at the Den, Northampton Town v Derby County at the PTS Academy Stadium, and Queens Park Rangers v Sheffield Wednesday at the Kiyan Prince Foundation Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Camouflaged spy cameras go 'undercover' in the animal world. For the first time, cameras follow a family of gorillas, with Spy Grub and Spy Fruit testing their intelligence. Elsewhere in Brazil, Spy Jaguar Cub explores a beautiful gathering of water birds, capybara and caiman, and joins Spy Caiman to film a feeding frenzy(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp takes the bargain hunting teams to Leominster, Herefordshire, where two teams compete to buy three items that will hopefully make the greater profit at auction. Providing a helping hand to the sides are experts Paul Laidlaw and Mark Ashley. Natasha also meets Royal Worcester aficionado Henry Sandon(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester City v Fulham (Kick-off 1.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the fourth-round tie at Etihad Stadium. Championship outfit Fulham have already eliminated one Premier League team when they overcame Aston Villa 2-1 at home. However, they face a far tougher task away to the holders, who beat Port Vale 4-1 in the previous round and comfortably defeated the Cottagers here in both the Premier League and Carabao Cup last season. WIth analysis from Micah Richards and Tim Cahill(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Seventy-five years after the liberation of Auschwitz, Aled Jones visits the National Holocaust Centre in Nottingham, while Pam Rhodes meets survivors, including some who were rescued by the Kindertransport. Plus, hymns from around the country including Abide with Me from Glasgow Cathedral, The Lord's My Shepherd from Tideswell in Derbyshire and Make Me a Channel Of Your Peace from Northampton(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton heads to the Welsh Borders to help a couple with a budget of £775,000 find a countryside home to raise their young son and establish a glamping retreat business(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Shrewsbury Town v Liverpool (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the fourth-round tie at Montgomery Waters Meadow. Defender Aaron Pierre's late strike in a replay against Championship side Bristol City earned the Shrews this lucrative clash. While the likelihood of Jurgen Klopp rotating a squad that is also chasing glory in the league and Europe may give the League One outfit some hope, the Reds were still able to overcome rivals Everton 1-0 in the previous round after making nine changes thanks to Curtis Jones' spectacular winner. With analysis from Ian Wright and Alan Shearer(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Trigger-happy space police the Judoon are targeting 21st-century Gloucester, so the Doctor, Yaz, Ryan and Graham race back to Earth in order to prevent them doing too much damage to the cathedral city. But who are the Judoon looking for and what did they do to incur their wrath? Guest starring Neil Stuke, Jo Martin and Ritu Arya(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne volunteers to put up four trainee doctors at Nonnatus House while they undertake the practical aspect of their obstetrics diploma, hoping the exercise will remind the council how vital the home is to the community and help remove the threat of demolition. There is a lot of excitement about the new arrivals and the doctors don't disappoint. On his CDC rounds, Fred comes across a street marked for demolition, with one house clearly showing signs of life. Period drama, starring Jenny Agutter(n)

programme.title: The Trial of Christine Keeler
programme.desc: Stephen's life hangs in the balance as his sentence is decided at the Old Bailey, while Christine's lie about Lucky Gordon haunts her and she faces trial for perjury. As Profumo rebuilds his reputation by working for an East London charity, and Mandy tries to make a career as a singer, Christine deals with the consequence of being labelled a liar and a prostitute, and reflects on happier days with Stephen and Mandy. Fact-based drama, starring Sophie Cookson, Ben Miles, James Norton and Ellie Bamber(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manish Bhasin presents action from today's fourth-round ties, including Manchester City v Fulham at Etihad Stadium and Shrewsbury v Liverpool at Montgomery Waters Meadow. The Citizens were continuing the defence of the trophy they lifted last season, while the Reds were expected to remain in contention to win the cup for the first time since 2006. With analysis from Danny Murphy and Martin Keown(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic hockey gold medallist Sam Quek, Great Britain 400m great Roger Black, Welsh rugby union star Gareth Anscombe and world triathlon champion Jonny Brownlee join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: It has long been believed that eating fat is unhealthy. But is it really as bad as people think? With recent headlines casting doubts on such fears, medic Saleyha Ahsan aims to cut through the confusion. She reveals startling new research that suggests some saturated fats might actually be good for people's health, invites volunteers to find out what happens to their bodies and minds if they stop eating it entirely, and discovers the fat hidden in seawater that could make the food of the future a lot healthier(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Crime - Are We Tough Enough?
programme.desc: Barrister Chris Daw and solicitor Ayesha Nayyar look at how Britain is handling law and order, beginning by examining the role of the courts. Chris argues that fewer people need to be in the system, while Ayesha thinks more people should be prosecuted and those found guilty should be sent to prison for longer(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Wallasey on Merseyside, Porth in South Wales, and Ripley in Derbyshire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Cassie Thorn is convinced moving to Australia will be perfect for her family, but her husband Aaron is not so sure, so they and their children sample life in Brisbane for a week(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A man is caught unawares by muggers on the way home after a night out, but his assailants get a surprise of their own, while a launderette owner is targeted by thieves(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell help teams search for items at the Peterborough Festival of Antiques, while Eric Knowles views a horseshoe collection at Oakham Castle, Rutland(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel becomes the new resident doctor inside the prison where Jimmi is being held, while Bear is behaving mysteriously and an old friend of Al's claims to have seen a ghost(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A man suspected of killing a police officer is seen near Kembleford and Mallory is determined to catch him, but he has to ask the sleuthing priest for help when disaster strikes(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Dorset to help a couple with £475,000 find a rural property that fits the bill when it comes to a workshop and providing space to keep hens. Jules also delves in the county's glove-making heritage and produces his own bespoke pair(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Farming friends Mark and Sean launch their Herefordshire-inspired crisps at the Droitwich Food Festival, while horticultural pioneer Sophie sells her Juneberry gin(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Louise Gostelow and David Harper scour Northern Ireland for items to sell at auction in Belfast, along the way visiting one of the world's oldest distilleries and sampling seaweed(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Getaways
programme.desc: Holly Hamilton and JJ Chalmers report on a dual-centre city break in the Czech Republic. They start out in the capital, Prague, before heading to Brno, the country's second city. Highlights include a river tour, a wall dedicated to the Beatles, one of the world's ugliest buildings and an art gallery with its very own airship. For the short break, actor Kristian Nairn, better known to Game of Thrones fans as gentle giant Hodor, visits Hillsborough Castle in Northern Ireland's County(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Leo continues to cause trouble on the market, but he soon regrets abusing Kat when she hits back at him and attacks his stall. Linda is upset when Mick does not stand up for her against gossips, the Panesars' mother Suki arrives in Walford and makes an immediate impression on Jean as she orders her sons about and Ian revels in his councillor win(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of miles of motorways are being made smart by turning the hard shoulder into a live lane, but many experts think they are dangerous for cars that have broken down in them. Richard Bilton speaks to the families of crash victims and asks whether Britain's motorways are becoming death traps(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Teenagers messing around in a stolen car collide with a pillar in a multistorey car park. The joyriders escape, but within the smashed concrete, the unmistakable form of a skull is revealed. After painstakingly extracting the skeleton encased inside, Nikki and the Lyell team must investigate a suspicious death from 20 years previously. Meanwhile, Clarissa faces a difficult decision regarding her mother's care. First of a two-part episode of the crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, David Caves, Richard Lintern and Liz Carr(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Documentary following the wedding preparations of Belfast woman Janine Howard, who was left with severe facial disfigurement after contracting a rare flesh-eating infection when she was a child(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: People said to be able to cure a host of common ailments using secret prayers and actions, meeting Sr Nora Smyth, Pat McGrade and Danny Gallagher to discover how this still prevails in a contemporary society(n)

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam is back at his parents' house in Leeds intending to prepare for a performance at a local spoken-word night, but instead procrastinates and plays old video games. But when he turns on his childhood computer and stumbles on an old home-made hip-hop track he created with his teenage mates, it triggers a memory(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Outspoken funnyman Frankie Boyle hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour before handing over to the acerbic Simon Evans and actress/stand-up Aisling Bea(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth and Ian Theasby serve up a plant-based lasagne, a vegan breakfast - including smoky barbecue beans, vegan scrambled eggs and a meat-free alternative to bacon - and salted caramel apple crumble with custard. The duo also try to convince meat-loving weatherman Alex Beresford that their big bosh burger is as good as the meaty ones he loves, and for their snack hacks, they show off their recipes for nut butters(n)

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Ben Fogle recalculates the height of England's highest mountain, Scafell Pike, with the team from the Ordnance Survey. Liz Bonnin visits Cheshire's Lyme Park to witness one of autumn's greatest natural wonders - the red deer rut. Plus, chef Gino D'Acampo invites viewers to his adopted home county of Hertfordshire to find woodland ingredients for his mushroom bruschetta(n)

programme.desc: The spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity coaches seek out the nation's best vocal talent. programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott explores Trinidad, where he visits a Hindu temple built on land reclaimed from the sea, prepares grilled tamarind tofu with callaloo pesto, and samples a local speciality - curried chickpeas in a fried bread roll. On a visit to Maracas Bay, he makes a coconut Caribbean chai(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth serves up his father's paella, he and Ian Theasby bake a Victoria sponge and they are joined by Day Radley, founder of the Vegan Chef School, who demonstrates her beetroot ravioli. Henry and Ian also show how to jazz up home-made houmous and make a Texan-style pizza, with spiced tofu and bourbon sauce(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Lose Weight
programme.desc: At a time of year when many people feel the pinch in both their wallets and their waistlines, the consumer health show returns, presented by Sian Williams and Ranj Singh. Over the series they compare 12 of the latest diets, beginning with the 16:8 intermittent fasting diet and a meal replacement shake plan. They also find out how it's possible to have a drink ruining blowing a diet, investigating some of the latest low-calorie and low-alcohol tipples on the market(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia visits the glamorous island of Santorini, beginning in one of its most exclusive hotels where she discovers how one of the Aegean's poorest islands became the playground for the rich and famous. Striking away from the bustling crowds, Julia heads inland for a walk on the wild side, hiking through a forager's paradise to the hilltop village of Pyrgos to watch the sun go down. After sampling a local delicacy made from fava beans, she heads out to sea to find out how local fishermen are joining forces with environmentalists to keep Santorini's pristine waters plastic-free(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: The spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity coaches seek out the nation's best vocal talent. But there is a simple twist - with the four coaches selecting their teams through 'blind auditions', looks play no part in the selection process, meaning each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone. Meghan Trainor is the newest recruit to the judging panel, joining series regulars Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Will.i.am(n)

programme.title: The Masked Singer
programme.desc: Donny Osmond joins regular panellists Rita Ora, Jonathan Ross and Davina McCall on this edition of the singing contest, as they attempt to guess the identities of the six famous faces performing from behind bizarre costumes. At the end of this week's show, a fifth singer will be unmasked and eliminated. Hosted by Joel Dommett(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a Coronation Street edition of the quiz, as celebrity contestants Shelley King, Daniel Brocklebank, Kym Marsh and Jack P Shepherd pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: The celebrities and their professional partners perform yet again, hoping to get a high score from the judges - not to mention plenty of viewer votes - and avoid following Trisha Goddard and Lucrezia Millarini in being eliminated from the contest. And while the votes are being counted, Jane Torvill and Christopher Dean take to the ice to show the contestants how it should be done. Presented by Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Cleaner Luke Sumner's body is discovered close to his flat in Newcastle, but pathology concludes that the killing blow could have occurred several hours before he succumbed to his injuries, prompting Vera to piece together the victim's final hours in order to investigate his murder. But Luke lived a simple life and had even severed all ties to the quaint rural village where he grew up - including his mother Carmel. Can she shed any light on her son's murder?(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: For the next leg of their journey, Bradley and Barney visit the Red River Gorge in Kentucky, before heading to the supposedly haunted Poasttown Elementary School in Middletown, Ohio. The next day, they travel to Louisville, birthplace of legendary boxer Muhammad Ali, where they follow in his footsteps by sparring at the local boxing gym. At the end of a long day, Barney rewards his dad with a well-earned meal at a KFC restaurant in Kentucky - the home state of the chain's founder Colonel Harland Sanders(n)

programme.title: The Metro: A Rail Life Story
programme.desc: Tens of thousands of Newcastle United fans pass through the Metro for a home game on the first day of the new football season - and many of them are far from pleased with the club's owner. A guide horse undergoes training on the Metro system before moving to London, and in Sunderland, a crackdown on fare dodgers gets underway(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura's worst fears come to pass and her hidden past is exposed, but she must fight to put her personal struggles aside for the sake of one of her pupils, whose physical distress throws her unexpectedly at the mercy of Andrew Earlham. Vanessa also finds her resolve shaken after an unsettling encounter with Andrew(n)

programme.title: Teach My Pet to Do That
programme.desc: A miniature horse and working cocker spaniel are taught how to shut multiple kitchen cupboard doors, and a raccoon demonstrates some of the tricks in its repertoire, including riding a bike and pushing a pram. Plus, the trainers hit the road to help a young girl scout train her pet rat Lily to do an agility course. Presented by Alexander Armstrong(n)

programme.desc: Major documentary telling the inside story of the royal family and the Queen's record-breaking reign. programme.title: Craig of the Creek

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth and Ian Theasby serve up a plant-based lasagne, a vegan breakfast - including smoky barbecue beans, vegan scrambled eggs and a meat-free alternative to bacon - and salted caramel apple crumble with custard. The duo also try to convince meat-loving weatherman Alex Beresford that their big bosh burger is as good as the meaty ones he loves, and for their snack hacks, they show off their recipes for nut butters(n)

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Ben Fogle recalculates the height of England's highest mountain, Scafell Pike, with the team from the Ordnance Survey. Liz Bonnin visits Cheshire's Lyme Park to witness one of autumn's greatest natural wonders - the red deer rut. Plus, chef Gino D'Acampo invites viewers to his adopted home county of Hertfordshire to find woodland ingredients for his mushroom bruschetta(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 2.25 Cotswold Steeple Chase and 3.35 Gallardhomes.com Cleeve Hurdle, and races at 1.50 and 3.00 from Cheltenham. Plus, races from 2.05, 2.40 and 3.15 at Doncaster. With analysis from Ruby Walsh and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Alice Plunkett, Matt Chapman, Oli Bell and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the show's most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shepherd, in which the the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as another four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking contestants Chris Hoy, Martine McCutcheon and Faye Tozer to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for their chosen charity(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: The spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity coaches seek out the nation's best vocal talent. But there is a simple twist - with the four coaches selecting their teams through 'blind auditions', looks play no part in the selection process, meaning each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone. Meghan Trainor is the newest recruit to the judging panel, joining series regulars Olly Murs, Tom Jones and Will.i.am(n)

programme.desc: A disorganised woman is asked to be maid of honour at her best friend's wedding. Taking charge of the prenuptial bridal celebrations, she resolves to give her pal and accompanying bridesmaids the time of their lives, but as her efforts slide into chaos she finds the wife of the groom's boss is keen to usurp her position. Comedy, with Kristen Wiig, Rose Byrne and Melissa McCarthy(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott explores Trinidad, where he visits a Hindu temple built on land reclaimed from the sea, prepares grilled tamarind tofu with callaloo pesto, and samples a local speciality - curried chickpeas in a fried bread roll. On a visit to Maracas Bay, he makes a coconut Caribbean chai(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth serves up his father's paella, he and Ian Theasby bake a Victoria sponge and they are joined by Day Radley, founder of the Vegan Chef School, who demonstrates her beetroot ravioli. Henry and Ian also show how to jazz up home-made houmous and make a Texan-style pizza, with spiced tofu and bourbon sauce(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Lose Weight
programme.desc: At a time of year when many people feel the pinch in both their wallets and their waistlines, the consumer health show returns, presented by Sian Williams and Ranj Singh. Over the series they compare 12 of the latest diets, beginning with the 16:8 intermittent fasting diet and a meal replacement shake plan. They also find out how it's possible to have a drink ruining blowing a diet, investigating some of the latest low-calorie and low-alcohol tipples on the market(n)

programme.title: The Greek Islands with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia visits the glamorous island of Santorini, beginning in one of its most exclusive hotels where she discovers how one of the Aegean's poorest islands became the playground for the rich and famous. Striking away from the bustling crowds, Julia heads inland for a walk on the wild side, hiking through a forager's paradise to the hilltop village of Pyrgos to watch the sun go down. After sampling a local delicacy made from fava beans, she heads out to sea to find out how local fishermen are joining forces with environmentalists to keep Santorini's pristine waters plastic-free(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: The spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity coaches seek out the nation's best vocal talent. programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a Coronation Street edition of the quiz, as celebrity contestants Shelley King, Daniel Brocklebank, Kym Marsh and Jack P Shepherd pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: The celebrities and their professional partners perform yet again, hoping to get a high score from the judges - not to mention plenty of viewer votes - and avoid following Trisha Goddard and Lucrezia Millarini in being eliminated from the contest. And while the votes are being counted, Jane Torvill and Christopher Dean take to the ice to show the contestants how it should be done. Presented by Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Cleaner Luke Sumner's body is discovered close to his flat in Newcastle, but pathology concludes that the killing blow could have occurred several hours before he succumbed to his injuries, prompting Vera to piece together the victim's final hours in order to investigate his murder. But Luke lived a simple life and had even severed all ties to the quaint rural village where he grew up - including his mother Carmel. Can she shed any light on her son's murder?(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: For the next leg of their journey, Bradley and Barney visit the Red River Gorge in Kentucky, before heading to the supposedly haunted Poasttown Elementary School in Middletown, Ohio. The next day, they travel to Louisville, birthplace of legendary boxer Muhammad Ali, where they follow in his footsteps by sparring at the local boxing gym. At the end of a long day, Barney rewards his dad with a well-earned meal at a KFC restaurant in Kentucky - the home state of the chain's founder Colonel Harland Sanders(n)

programme.title: The Metro: A Rail Life Story
programme.desc: Tens of thousands of Newcastle United fans pass through the Metro for a home game on the first day of the new football season - and many of them are far from pleased with the club's owner. A guide horse undergoes training on the Metro system before moving to London, and in Sunderland, a crackdown on fare dodgers gets underway(n)

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura's worst fears come to pass and her hidden past is exposed, but she must fight to put her personal struggles aside for the sake of one of her pupils, whose physical distress throws her unexpectedly at the mercy of Andrew Earlham. Vanessa also finds her resolve shaken after an unsettling encounter with Andrew(n)

programme.title: Teach My Pet to Do That
programme.desc: A miniature horse and working cocker spaniel are taught how to shut multiple kitchen cupboard doors, and a raccoon demonstrates some of the tricks in its repertoire, including riding a bike and pushing a pram. Plus, the trainers hit the road to help a young girl scout train her pet rat Lily to do an agility course. Presented by Alexander Armstrong(n)

programme.desc: Stewie auditions in drag for his favourite TV show after discovering it is only searching for girls, and soon becomes a hit with viewers. Meg shocks the entire city by dating a conventionally attractive boy(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: The CIA teaches Stan about 'rubbernecking', the art of checking out other women away from the gaze of their wives. However, after the training, Stan gets into an accident while attempting to take a photo of an attractive jogger. Meanwhile, Roger and Klaus try to hide a wine stain on the new sofa(n)

programme.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol 2 (2017)
programme.desc: After saving Xandar from Ronan's wrath, intergalactic outlaws Peter Quill (AKA Star-Lord), Gamora, Drax the Destroyer, Groot and Rocket Raccoon are recognised as heroes. Now, they must fight to stay together as they unravel the mystery surrounding Ego, a man who claims to be Quill's father. Writer/director James Gunn's follow up to his 2014 blockbuster, which reunites Chris Pratt, Zoe Saldana, Karen Gillan and Dave Bautista, with support from Kurt Russell, Sylvester Stallone and Pom Klementieff. Bradley Cooper also provides the voice of Rocket and Vin Diesel is Groot(n)

programme.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End (2007)
programme.desc: The swashbuckling rogues join forces with old foe Barbossa to take on villainous Lord Beckett of the East India Trading Company, who plans to extinguish piracy forever. But first they have to journey to the ends of the Earth to free Captain Jack Sparrow from his hellish imprisonment in Davy Jones' Locker. When the group return from their rescue mission, they join forces with the nine Pirate Lords to fight for their freedom once and for all. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Johnny Depp, Geoffrey Rush, Orlando Bloom, Keira Knightley and Chow Yun-Fat, and featuring a cameo appearance by Keith Richards(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Trying to rebound from his recent separation, Walden goes looking for Mrs Right. Meanwhile, Alan has an opportunity to reconcile with his on-again, off-again girlfriend Lyndsey. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan is mortified to discover Lyndsey has entered the adult film industry, and decides to confront her about her chosen career. However, the encounter only results in the exposure of his own secrets, so Walden tries to lift his spirits by hosting a party(n)

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Laura Whitmore presents as the third week of the relationship-based reality show begins with plenty more surprises in store as the singletons compete to pair off in Cape Town(n)

programme.title: Love Island: Aftersun
programme.desc: Laura Whitmore is joined by a celebrity panel of Love Island superfans to dissect all the news from the villa. Plus, interviews with the latest islanders to be dumped(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the murder of a businesswoman connected with an organisation that supports gifted children, who had apparently been blackmailing the father of one particularly promising youngster. Further deaths follow, and as the body count rises, they realise there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case. Matters are complicated still further by the presence in Oxford of former MI5 spymaster Grace Orde, who is visiting to sign copies of her tell-all memoir - which provides a vital clue to the killer's identity. Detective drama, guest starring Anna Chancellor and Cherie Lunghi, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tyrone and Evelyn unravel Jade's web of lies. Later, when Yasmeen finds Jade lying unconscious in the ginnel, Fiz looks like the prime suspect(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A farmer's murder and the crash landing of an enemy plane lead Foyle to investigate the activities of a group of land girls, and the detective begins to suspect the captured German airmen have something to hide. Stella Gonet and James Wilby guest star in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A crackdown on an organised crime racket leads Foyle and Milner to a shipyard owned by the Talbot brothers, a duo with dubious links to a communist agitator who has recently moved to the area. When an engineer is murdered just hours after working at the shipyard, the detectives desperately try to track down the killer and discover the truth surrounding the Talbots' shady business dealings. Peter Capaldi guest stars in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: American GIs arrive in Hastings to a mixed welcome and Foyle is called on to calm the locals. But his attempts at soothing relations are jeopardised when a barmaid's involvement with a visiting soldier results in murder, leading the detective to defy orders to keep the peace and solve the case. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks and Keith Barron(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An international cycling competition comes to the Midsomer village of Burwood Mantle, but when the race leader is murdered, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson must investigate. During the race's finale, the victim overtook his team-mate to win, leaving the team owner annoyed that he disobeyed his orders, and the rival manager disappointed that his team lost. However, as the pair look into the case, they find themselves drawn into a competitive world of blackmail, bribery and bloodshed, where winning comes at any cost(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Art comes to the Midsomer village of Angel's Rise with the opening of a new sculpture park, but when its launch is marred by murder, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are called to investigate. However, they must deal with an apparently unexplainable broken locket found near the scene of the crime, a host of suspects, and a complex web of deception. As the crime solving duo inspect further, they find they have to get creative to solve a case where art imitates death and everything has a deeper meaning(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. After seven years of ducking the issue, Lewis and Hobson are embarking on a relationship, but the detective also has a new case to solve. Elderly don Richard Seager is murdered on the night of his release from prison, having served a sentence for causing death by dangerous driving. Crushed by a classic car, Seager mysteriously scratched the number 500 into its paintwork before his demise, and the academic's wife suspects a member of his victim's family is responsible for the killing. With Stephen Churchett, Alison Steadman and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall helps Elizabeth Wells and her daughter Janice track down the son Elizabeth put up for adoption 50 years earlier. She also offers expert assistance to Tracey Collins, who discovered at the age of 17 that she has a brother, Thomas, who she has never met. Tracey's father had vowed to help her find him, but died before he was able to share crucial information about his location. Now, Tracey seeks guidance from Nicky Campbell in tracking Thomas down(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp

programme.title: Rising Damp

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby tries to drum up support for his local election candidate, but finds Alan and Philip have other ideas about who should represent their interests. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington star(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A local woman is killed when an experiment in biological warfare goes tragically wrong. Sam also falls victim to a potentially fatal illness as a result of the project. Foyle is forced to head into the most secretive and dangerous areas of wartime research, unearthing secrets that cannot be revealed even to the prime minister. Michael Kitchen stars, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Philip Franks(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When Grace Phillips is killed in an explosion in a munitions factory, Foyle is asked by one of her co-workers to investigate the death. Initially he concludes a tragic accident has occurred until another woman is found murdered and Foyle starts to believe the deaths are connected. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Lewis

Detective drama, guest starring Anna Chancellor and Cherie Lunghi, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets a trio of canine cases who have kept the Battersea medical team busy. First up is Peanut the pug, a boisterous and playful dog who has a serious breathing problem which means he needs a major operation. Mystery mutt Lacey was found wandering the streets with no identification. However, a check-up soon reveals a shocking clue to her past. Paul also meets Coco, a chocolate Labrador who is suffering from severe heatstroke. If she cannot be cooled down quickly enough, she could face serious medical consequences - a situation that isn't helped by the fact that she is overweight(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Battersea plays host to a big name this week when A-list actor Tom Hardy drops in for a tour, so Paul shows him round, introducing him to boisterous American bulldog Titch. But can he persuade Tom to take the mammoth mutt home with him? The presenter also meets nervous staffie Sydney, who is well-known to the home as his face was used on a poster from a previous stay five years earlier, and pampered shih-tzu Millie, who was dropped off with a wardrobe of her favourite clothes(n)

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Part two of two. The documentary examines Charles's legacy for the Duchy, as senior staff prepare William for the day that he succeeds his father as the next Duke of Cornwall. The documentary also examines his well-known passion for trees and traditional rural crafts, and how these may shape the Duchy's future(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ruby becomes personally involved in the case of an abandoned baby that is brought to the hospital during the Holi festival, and Lydia is reunited with an expat artist, whose unusual lifestyle may be about to catch up with him. Medical drama set in coastal town in tropical South India, starring Amrita Acharia and Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ram clashes with Lydia as a medical case becomes personal on the day he is due to attend a lavish wedding. Ruby treats a patient from the UK whose story does not add up, and the experience leaves her wondering where she really belongs(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: A royal visit to a family owned munitions factory puts a spring in the step of Chief Superintendent Bright, but Morse is still stuck on general duties and excluded from the event. However, when an unpopular worker is found murdered in a secluded area of the shop floor, the detective finds himself drawn into the affairs of the company and its dysfunctional owners. The prime suspect is a colleague who had fallen out with the victim over an industrial accident, and Bright is eager for the investigation to be carried out as quickly and quietly as possible, but Endeavour is not convinced the case can be solved so easily. Shaun Evans stars, with Martin Jarvis, Jenny Seagrove and Craig Parkinson(n)

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels along the Staffordshire and Worcestershire canal, widely regarded as one of the oldest and prettiest waterways in Britain. On his journey he marvels at ingenious 18th-century engineering, tackles the difficult skill of glassblowing and discovers a secret underground cold war bunker(n)

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin(n)

programme.desc: A week-long storyline takes place during the opening of Hawkford Outdoor Pursuits. It finishes with Graham's day, and a series of flashbacks revealing the truth(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre takes two weeks off work and cleans the house ready for Samir. Percy encourages Curly to court Paula. Tanya asks Des to drop her from the case(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre fears Samir will have gone off her, but he is thrilled to see her. Vera tells Curly she is disgusted with him leering after Paula. Martin confiscates Nick's video games(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It is the morning after and the McAllisters wake up to a nightmare. Eric Pollard's plans are threatened by bad publicity, while Ned Glover receives a welcome offer(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The full extent of Luke's predicament is revealed when the McAllisters receive shocking news. Turner struggles alone at the Woolpack, while Ned Glover has something to tell Jan(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Convicted murderer Henry Stoddard's release from prison coincides with a spate of vandalism, and locals presume he is responsible for the petty crime. But when the former jailbird insists he did not kill PC Machin all those years ago, Walker reopens the case and races to stop Machin's son exacting revenge. Meanwhile, Peggy sees the chance to make some money when her birth certificate suggests she is related to Lord Ashfordly(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby invests in some new furniture in a desperate attempt to impress Ruth's replacement, Brenda, but makes the mistake of opting to pay for it on the never-never. Leonard Rossiter and Gay Rose star(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby manages to frighten Alan by kidding him the house is haunted by a spectral 'grey lady' - but ends up getting a taste of his own medicine. Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale star(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin find a rare plant in the Ropers' garden. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective faces a tough time while looking into a murder at a peaceful college. After suffering the familiar pangs of unrequited love, he clashes with the unpleasant college master and then unwittingly provides his main suspects with a watertight alibi. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Robert Stephens, Amanda Burton and Philip Middlemiss(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Local boy Barry Dyson is accused of a series of arson attacks - and his clandestine relationship with a former employer's daughter complicates his plea of innocence. Peggy's latest money-making scheme with old friend Denzil creates an eyesore, forcing Jack to use his artistic flair to rectify matters, before he and Blaketon resort to more drastic - and illegal - measures. Rural drama, starring Gwen Taylor and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Pollard's plotting provokes opposition and Kathy finds a shoulder to cry on. Jan Glover is offered a job, while circumstances cause Luke to lose his freedom(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The outlook for Luke looks grim while his parents run the gauntlet of village gossip. Zoe has a staffing problem and Betty tells all about her summer of love(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick tells Martin he is never going to do anything he says. Mike is stunned to learn that Maggie is getting married. The Robertses go to France(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des worries that Tanya will lie in court, and Mike pressures Alma into letting Mark live with them. Alf gets on well with the French mayor, until Fred throws a spanner in the works(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Larry goes to Athens on a short trip, and returns with a royal guest. Louisa is delighted to have a prince staying under her roof, but soon finds herself with a less welcome visitor in the shape of Captain Creech. Tension is soon stirred up between the two houseguests when Gerry sits an interview for a local school, and the prince and the captain voice very different opinions on the subject of education(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Local boy Barry Dyson is accused of a series of arson attacks - and his clandestine relationship with a former employer's daughter complicates his plea of innocence. Peggy's latest money-making scheme with old friend Denzil creates an eyesore, forcing Jack to use his artistic flair to rectify matters, before he and Blaketon resort to more drastic - and illegal - measures. Rural drama, starring Gwen Taylor and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's god-daughter Lydia and her son James - who is severely traumatised by a bombing at his school - come to stay unexpectedly. Struggling to look after the lad when his mother goes missing, Foyle's life suffers a further complication when the body of a local man is found near a military centre - leading him into the top-secret world of weapons research. Kate Fleetwood and Michael Jayston guest star, with Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As the sleuth studies for his forthcoming sergeant's exam, he investigates a hit-and-run that claimed the life of an eminent Oxford professor. The victim had been at odds with his peers over the fate of a potentially lucrative parcel of college-owned land, and the case is complicated by the appearance of DI Thursday's old gangland enemy - whom he must face alone while Endeavour makes a trip back to Lincolnshire to visit his ailing father(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Georgia Taylor (Coronation Street, Casualty) and Paterson Joseph (Peep Show) join the cast as the crime drama returns for its seventh series. In the first of a two-part story, a suicidal man causes a train crash when he drives onto a railway crossing, killing 15 people. With Jake prosecuting, the man's defence barrister tries to prove his vulnerability and mental illness, but then a twist in the investigation leaves Sam's career hanging in the balance. Starring Bradley Walsh, Dominic Rowan and Paul Nicholls(n)
