programme.desc: Miss Jones reports a prowler to the police and it is not long before an inspector calls to investigate - but, unfortunately for Rigsby, he is not all he seems. George Sewell guest stars in the classic comedy, with Leonard Rossiter and Frances de la Tour(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby tries to drum up support for his local election candidate, but finds Alan and Philip have other ideas about who should represent their interests. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington star(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tyrone and Evelyn unravel Jade's web of lies. Later, when Yasmeen finds Jade lying unconscious in the ginnel, Fiz looks like the prime suspect(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A week-long storyline takes place during the opening of Hawkford Outdoor Pursuits. It finishes with Graham's day, and a series of flashbacks revealing the truth(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A local woman is killed when an experiment in biological warfare goes tragically wrong. Sam also falls victim to a potentially fatal illness as a result of the project. Foyle is forced to head into the most secretive and dangerous areas of wartime research, unearthing secrets that cannot be revealed even to the prime minister. Michael Kitchen stars, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Philip Franks(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When Grace Phillips is killed in an explosion in a munitions factory, Foyle is asked by one of her co-workers to investigate the death. Initially he concludes a tragic accident has occurred until another woman is found murdered and Foyle starts to believe the deaths are connected. Wartime drama, starring Michael Kitchen(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the murder of a businesswoman connected with an organisation that supports gifted children, who had apparently been blackmailing the father of one particularly promising youngster. Further deaths follow, and as the body count rises, they realise there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case. Matters are complicated still further by the presence in Oxford of former MI5 spymaster Grace Orde, who is visiting to sign copies of her tell-all memoir - which provides a vital clue to the killer's identity. Detective drama, guest starring Anna Chancellor and Cherie Lunghi, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets a trio of canine cases who have kept the Battersea medical team busy. First up is Peanut the pug, a boisterous and playful dog who has a serious breathing problem which means he needs a major operation. Mystery mutt Lacey was found wandering the streets with no identification. However, a check-up soon reveals a shocking clue to her past. Paul also meets Coco, a chocolate Labrador who is suffering from severe heatstroke. If she cannot be cooled down quickly enough, she could face serious medical consequences - a situation that isn't helped by the fact that she is overweight(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Battersea plays host to a big name this week when A-list actor Tom Hardy drops in for a tour, so Paul shows him round, introducing him to boisterous American bulldog Titch. But can he persuade Tom to take the mammoth mutt home with him? The presenter also meets nervous staffie Sydney, who is well-known to the home as his face was used on a poster from a previous stay five years earlier, and pampered shih-tzu Millie, who was dropped off with a wardrobe of her favourite clothes(n)

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Part two of two. The documentary examines Charles's legacy for the Duchy, as senior staff prepare William for the day that he succeeds his father as the next Duke of Cornwall. The documentary also examines his well-known passion for trees and traditional rural crafts, and how these may shape the Duchy's future(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ruby becomes personally involved in the case of an abandoned baby that is brought to the hospital during the Holi festival, and Lydia is reunited with an expat artist, whose unusual lifestyle may be about to catch up with him. Medical drama set in coastal town in tropical South India, starring Amrita Acharia and Amanda Redman(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ram clashes with Lydia as a medical case becomes personal on the day he is due to attend a lavish wedding. Ruby treats a patient from the UK whose story does not add up, and the experience leaves her wondering where she really belongs(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: A royal visit to a family owned munitions factory puts a spring in the step of Chief Superintendent Bright, but Morse is still stuck on general duties and excluded from the event. However, when an unpopular worker is found murdered in a secluded area of the shop floor, the detective finds himself drawn into the affairs of the company and its dysfunctional owners. The prime suspect is a colleague who had fallen out with the victim over an industrial accident, and Bright is eager for the investigation to be carried out as quickly and quietly as possible, but Endeavour is not convinced the case can be solved so easily. Shaun Evans stars, with Martin Jarvis, Jenny Seagrove and Craig Parkinson(n)

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels along the Staffordshire and Worcestershire canal, widely regarded as one of the oldest and prettiest waterways in Britain. On his journey he marvels at ingenious 18th-century engineering, tackles the difficult skill of glassblowing and discovers a secret underground cold war bunker(n)

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: A week-long storyline takes place during the opening of Hawkford Outdoor Pursuits. It finishes with Graham's day, and a series of flashbacks revealing the truth(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre takes two weeks off work and cleans the house ready for Samir. Percy encourages Curly to court Paula. Tanya asks Des to drop her from the case(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre fears Samir will have gone off her, but he is thrilled to see her. Vera tells Curly she is disgusted with him leering after Paula. Martin confiscates Nick's video games(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It is the morning after and the McAllisters wake up to a nightmare. Eric Pollard's plans are threatened by bad publicity, while Ned Glover receives a welcome offer(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The full extent of Luke's predicament is revealed when the McAllisters receive shocking news. Turner struggles alone at the Woolpack, while Ned Glover has something to tell Jan(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Convicted murderer Henry Stoddard's release from prison coincides with a spate of vandalism, and locals presume he is responsible for the petty crime. But when the former jailbird insists he did not kill PC Machin all those years ago, Walker reopens the case and races to stop Machin's son exacting revenge. Meanwhile, Peggy sees the chance to make some money when her birth certificate suggests she is related to Lord Ashfordly(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby invests in some new furniture in a desperate attempt to impress Ruth's replacement, Brenda, but makes the mistake of opting to pay for it on the never-never. Leonard Rossiter and Gay Rose star(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby manages to frighten Alan by kidding him the house is haunted by a spectral 'grey lady' - but ends up getting a taste of his own medicine. Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale star(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin find a rare plant in the Ropers' garden. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective faces a tough time while looking into a murder at a peaceful college. After suffering the familiar pangs of unrequited love, he clashes with the unpleasant college master and then unwittingly provides his main suspects with a watertight alibi. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Robert Stephens, Amanda Burton and Philip Middlemiss(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Local boy Barry Dyson is accused of a series of arson attacks - and his clandestine relationship with a former employer's daughter complicates his plea of innocence. Peggy's latest money-making scheme with old friend Denzil creates an eyesore, forcing Jack to use his artistic flair to rectify matters, before he and Blaketon resort to more drastic - and illegal - measures. Rural drama, starring Gwen Taylor and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Pollard's plotting provokes opposition and Kathy finds a shoulder to cry on. Jan Glover is offered a job, while circumstances cause Luke to lose his freedom(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The outlook for Luke looks grim while his parents run the gauntlet of village gossip. Zoe has a staffing problem and Betty tells all about her summer of love(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick tells Martin he is never going to do anything he says. Mike is stunned to learn that Maggie is getting married. The Robertses go to France(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Des worries that Tanya will lie in court, and Mike pressures Alma into letting Mark live with them. Alf gets on well with the French mayor, until Fred throws a spanner in the works(n)

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: Larry goes to Athens on a short trip, and returns with a royal guest. Louisa is delighted to have a prince staying under her roof, but soon finds herself with a less welcome visitor in the shape of Captain Creech. Tension is soon stirred up between the two houseguests when Gerry sits an interview for a local school, and the prince and the captain voice very different opinions on the subject of education(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Local boy Barry Dyson is accused of a series of arson attacks - and his clandestine relationship with a former employer's daughter complicates his plea of innocence. Peggy's latest money-making scheme with old friend Denzil creates an eyesore, forcing Jack to use his artistic flair to rectify matters, before he and Blaketon resort to more drastic - and illegal - measures. Rural drama, starring Gwen Taylor and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The detective's god-daughter Lydia and her son James - who is severely traumatised by a bombing at his school - come to stay unexpectedly. Struggling to look after the lad when his mother goes missing, Foyle's life suffers a further complication when the body of a local man is found near a military centre - leading him into the top-secret world of weapons research. Kate Fleetwood and Michael Jayston guest star, with Michael Kitchen and Honeysuckle Weeks(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: As the sleuth studies for his forthcoming sergeant's exam, he investigates a hit-and-run that claimed the life of an eminent Oxford professor. The victim had been at odds with his peers over the fate of a potentially lucrative parcel of college-owned land, and the case is complicated by the appearance of DI Thursday's old gangland enemy - whom he must face alone while Endeavour makes a trip back to Lincolnshire to visit his ailing father(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Georgia Taylor (Coronation Street, Casualty) and Paterson Joseph (Peep Show) join the cast as the crime drama returns for its seventh series. In the first of a two-part story, a suicidal man causes a train crash when he drives onto a railway crossing, killing 15 people. With Jake prosecuting, the man's defence barrister tries to prove his vulnerability and mental illness, but then a twist in the investigation leaves Sam's career hanging in the balance. Starring Bradley Walsh, Dominic Rowan and Paul Nicholls(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman put a selection of TV experts through their paces in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz show, asking them to team up with colleagues and contemporaries to provide the least-likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The House That £100K Built presenter Kieran Long teams up with fellow architecture specialist Piers Taylor, while Homes Under the Hammer host Martin Roberts joins forces with Selling Houses' Amanda Lamb. programme.title: Flash Gordon (1980)
programme.desc: American football quarterback Flash Gordon and journalist Dale Arden find themselves aboard a rocket ship designed by scientist Dr Zarkov, who intends to travel across space to locate the source of recent natural disasters. The three arrive on the planet Mongo, where they soon learn that the attacks are the responsibility of evil emperor Ming the Merciless, who is planning to destroy the Earth. Sci-fi comic-strip adventure, with Sam J Jones, Max von Sydow, Brian Blessed, Melody Anderson, Topol, Timothy Dalton, Ornella Muti, Peter Wyngarde and Mariangela Melato. Featuring music by Queen(n)

However, with kick-off looming, one of Arthur's incompetent associates appears with a huge sum of money stolen from a rival businessman, leaving the the dodgy dealer seeking a way to stash the cash without it being noticed. George Cole and Gary Webster star(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur finds it increasingly hard to sell his dodgy second-hand cars and decides it might be more profitable to rent them out instead. However, panic ensues when his beloved Bentley is stolen. Meanwhile, Ray faces the possibility that his new girlfriend is involved with a gang of burglars. Drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A frustrated housewife supplies Regan with the names of the gang who recently ransacked a bookmakers - including that of her husband, a notorious robber. However, he has a cast-iron alibi, so proving his guilt proves harder than anticipated. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan's holiday is disrupted by the escape of a violent psychopath from prison. Tim Cook, dubbed 'The Leopard' by the press has vowed murderous revenge on the detective inspector for his earlier capture, and has friends on the outside prepared to harbour him while he plans his attack. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes and Dr Watson protect the last remaining member of the aristocratic Baskervilles, who believes his family is being haunted by a monstrous spectral hound roaming the remote Devon moors. Feature-length mystery based on Conan Doyle's classic story, starring Jeremy Brett, Edward Hardwicke, Ronald Pickup and Kristoffer Tabori(n)

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: The newly promoted lieutenant accepts a diplomatic commission as the war reaches a stalemate, and finds himself on French soil in the midst of a daring plot to overthrow the government. Swashbuckling adventure, starring Ioan Gruffudd, Robert Lindsay and Antony Sher(n)

programme.title: Red River (1948)
programme.desc: Conflict grows between a tough Texan rancher and his Civil War veteran foster son during a 1,000 mile cattle drive to Missouri. The older man's uncompromising methods and unyielding outlook lead his workers to mutiny - and the son ends up siding with the rebellious ranch hands against his father. Howard Hawks' Western, starring John Wayne, Montgomery Clift, Walter Brennan, Joanne Dru and Coleen Gray(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur and Ray's dreams of riches Down Under fall apart. Left penniless, Ray is forced to bar work while Arthur encounters roguish street trader Derek Collins. George Cole and Gary Webster star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 is assigned to protect an Middle Eastern diplomat who is being pursued by assassins. Bodie and Doyle find themselves relentlessly pursued by a gang of assailants that seems able to accurately anticipate their every move, leading to a tense car chase and a riverside shoot-out. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan and Carter track down those responsible for killing a driver and injuring a policeman while making off with vehicles carrying brandy on two separate occasions. Carter's new girlfriend, rookie cop Julie Kingdom, pursues a known fence and recovers the brandy. However, when Regan wants to take the credit, he and Carter come to blows. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The crime-busting coroner helps defend a college sports coach accused of indirectly causing the death of a potential Olympic champion(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo is convinced that a hit-and-run incident which killed a police chemist was deliberately planned, and discovers that an imprisoned corrupt cop and his son are plotting revenge(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A woman asks Holmes to search for her missing husband, a businessman who was last seen peering from the window of an opium den in London's notorious Limehouse area. Suspicion falls on a well-known local beggar - but his innocence is eventually proved in bizarre fashion. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur pawns his wife's jewellery to solve a temporary cash-flow problem _ but soon finds himself in deeper trouble than ever. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole as shady London entrepreneur Arthur Daley(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie's former girlfriend visits from East Germany and he finds himself framed for carrying out an assassination attempt - leaving Doyle with no choice but to bring him in for questioning. Drama, starring Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A member of a group of convicts slips off the roof and dies during a robbery. The apparent leader of the gang appears to be having doubts about his life and Regan senses he is deliberately pointing clues in a desire to be caught during his next job. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as impressionist Rory Bremner, weather reporter Laura Tobin, Dragons' Den's Deborah Meaden and TV gardener and chat show host Alan Titchmarsh pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: A walker on Croagh Patrick Mountain sustains a serious head injury and members of the Mayo Mountain Rescue Team carry him down the mountainside to the Irish Coast Guard helicopter. Cairngorm Mountain Rescue Team are called to a climber who has fallen several feet on a rocky outcrop known as Lurchers Crag, while a world-class surfer at Mullaghmore sustains a severe leg injury(n)

programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: In the north of England severe flooding, the worst in 70 years, brings chaos to the region. Standard emergency services are overwhelmed, and numerous volunteer rescue teams are brought in to assist in evacuating members of the public from their flooded homes. In Co Kerry, the local mountain rescue team is called to Torc Mountain and a steep path known as 'cardiac steps' to attend a walker complaining of chest pains. In the Cairngorm mountains, members of the local rescue team comb the third region to find a lost walker who has not returned home after heading out on a trek(n)

programme.title: Fighter Pilot: The Real Top Gun
programme.desc: The three students face the final aerial combat and mission tests to decide if they are ready to take to the skies in the new F35 Lightning fighter jet. The squadron they hope to join returns to Britain from training in South Carolina, and junior pilot Liam must master one of the most difficult manoeuvres he will ever have to face - mid air refuelling(n)

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day (1991)
programme.desc: A reprogrammed killing machine is sent back in time to the 20th century to protect John Connor, a teenage boy whose destiny is to save the human race. After breaking John's mother out of a mental institution, the pair set out to avert a future nuclear war - pursued by an advanced prototype terminator with shape-changing powers. Sci-fi action adventure sequel, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Linda Hamilton, Edward Furlong and Robert Patrick(n)

programme.title: Flash Gordon (1980)
programme.desc: American football quarterback Flash Gordon and journalist Dale Arden find themselves aboard a rocket ship designed by scientist Dr Zarkov, who intends to travel across space to locate the source of recent natural disasters. The three arrive on the planet Mongo, where they soon learn that the attacks are the responsibility of evil emperor Ming the Merciless, who is planning to destroy the Earth. Sci-fi comic-strip adventure, with Sam J Jones, Max von Sydow, Brian Blessed, Melody Anderson, Topol, Timothy Dalton, Ornella Muti, Peter Wyngarde and Mariangela Melato. Featuring music by Queen(n)

George Cole and Gary Webster star(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur finds it increasingly hard to sell his dodgy second-hand cars and decides it might be more profitable to rent them out instead. However, panic ensues when his beloved Bentley is stolen. Meanwhile, Ray faces the possibility that his new girlfriend is involved with a gang of burglars. Drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A frustrated housewife supplies Regan with the names of the gang who recently ransacked a bookmakers - including that of her husband, a notorious robber. However, he has a cast-iron alibi, so proving his guilt proves harder than anticipated. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan's holiday is disrupted by the escape of a violent psychopath from prison. Tim Cook, dubbed 'The Leopard' by the press has vowed murderous revenge on the detective inspector for his earlier capture, and has friends on the outside prepared to harbour him while he plans his attack. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes and Dr Watson protect the last remaining member of the aristocratic Baskervilles, who believes his family is being haunted by a monstrous spectral hound roaming the remote Devon moors. Feature-length mystery based on Conan Doyle's classic story, starring Jeremy Brett, Edward Hardwicke, Ronald Pickup and Kristoffer Tabori(n)

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: The newly promoted lieutenant accepts a diplomatic commission as the war reaches a stalemate, and finds himself on French soil in the midst of a daring plot to overthrow the government. Swashbuckling adventure, starring Ioan Gruffudd, Robert Lindsay and Antony Sher(n)

programme.title: Red River (1948)
programme.desc: Conflict grows between a tough Texan rancher and his Civil War veteran foster son during a 1,000 mile cattle drive to Missouri. The older man's uncompromising methods and unyielding outlook lead his workers to mutiny - and the son ends up siding with the rebellious ranch hands against his father. Howard Hawks' Western, starring John Wayne, Montgomery Clift, Walter Brennan, Joanne Dru and Coleen Gray(n)

To make matters worse, the kitchen is run by a chef called David Blaine who shares nothing of his famous namesake's magic when it comes to cooking - and whose threatening behaviour regularly gets him into trouble(n)

Their success meant they could afford to purchase the location next door in the 1990s, naming it Mama Maria after John's mother, who died in 2002. But when he lost his father as well, John became overwhelmed by the responsibility of running both establishments and his livelihood is at risk(n)

programme.title: Bring Back the Bush: Where Did Our Pubic Hair Go?

Along the way, she challenges herself and her peers to grow out their pubic hair and put on an exhibition in which they reveal their naked, natural bodies to the world(n)

programme.title: Meet the Drug Lords: Inside the Real Narcos

programme.title: Digging Up Britain's Past
programme.desc: Centuries ago, canals were the liquid highways of Britain, transporting raw materials and finished goods all over the country. Their arrival kick-started the Industrial Revolution, which was to turn Britain into a superpower. The very first water superhighway was the Bridgewater Canal, completed in 1761 to carry coal to Manchester from the mines in Worsley. Here, a team of archaeologists from Salford University roots out this industrial past, working with volunteers to excavate the very coal mine that inspired the creation of the canal(n)

programme.title: Tony Robinson's History of Britain
programme.desc: The actor meets Eastender Babs Clark, who was just eight when the Second World War began. Like so many, Babs depended on the newly built Bethnal Green Tube station as an air raid shelter during the Blitz. It was akin to an underground town, boasting a library, doctor and community hall. However, tragedy struck during one night raid, and her family was lucky to get out alive. The presenter also meets Maisie Lanyon, who was just five years old when the Germans invaded Guernsey(n)

programme.title: How the Victorians Built Britain
programme.desc: Michael Buerk discovers how the Victorians spent their leisure time, heading to Blackpool to learn how the seaside mini-break was transformed from a perk of the wealthy to a British institution. He steps onto one of the town's famous piers to learn how, thanks to architect and civil engineer Eugenius Birch, these humble loading aids became entertainment hubs. Michael also hears how a rivalry between two showbusiness giants resulted in some of the area's most glorious architecture and how the advent of electricity fuelled the town's tourist industry, via its world famous illuminations(n)

programme.title: Henry VIII and His Six Wives
programme.desc: Suzannah Lipscomb and Dan Jones examine the relationships between the king and his final four wives. Jane Seymour gave him the son and heir he craved, but died days after giving birth, and Henry was persuaded to marry again, discovering too late that Holbein's painting of Anne of Cleves was deceptive. Then he married the young and beautiful - but indiscreet - Catherine Howard, before the mature and wise Catherine Parr, who nearly lost her head when she pushed him too far on religious reform(n)

programme.title: Elizabeth: The Golden Age (2007)
programme.desc: Queen Elizabeth I faces threats at home and abroad, as Mary, Queen of Scots plots against her while the King of Spain builds a vast armada to undertake an invasion of England. Unable to reconcile her feelings for the dashing Sir Walter Raleigh with her duty to her country, the monarch engineers a romance between the courtier and one of her ladies-in-waiting. Historical drama sequel, with Cate Blanchett, Clive Owen, Geoffrey Rush and Samantha Morton(n)

programme.title: The Queen Mother
programme.desc: Documentary exploring pivotal moments in the queen mother's life, starting with the tumultuous events of 1936. She began the year as Duchess of York, but following the abdication of her brother-in-law Edward VIII, her husband George was forced to take on the throne, leaving her unexpectedly thrust into the role of queen and a vital source of support for the unprepared new monarch(n)

programme.title: Royals in Wartime
programme.desc: Part one of two. Many members of the royal family have seen action on the frontline in some of Britain's most significant conflicts. Cameras reveal how the House of Windsor risked life and limb to help the nation through the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Inside Iceland: Britain's Budget Supermarket
programme.desc: A current area for growth is vegan food, so Iceland's vegan buyer visits a factory to watch meatless sausage rolls being produced, as well as scoping out vegan products for the future. The Iceland team also tucks into its rivals' choc ices, while out in Italy the team develops a new ice-cream cake. Elsewhere, some environmentally friendly packaging for fruit and vegetables is put to the test with a view to making the company plastic free by 2023(n)

programme.title: The Wonderful World of Chocolate
programme.desc: Food giant Nestlé opens the doors of its Perugia factory in Italy, where production manager Davide looks into the secrets of what is undeniably a labour of love. Chocolatier Prudence Staite has a big challenge ahead, creating a full-blown chocolate replica of a much-loved vintage motorbike for the National Motorcycle Museum in Solihull, as it celebrates its 35th birthday(n)

programme.title: An Officer and a Gentleman (1982)
programme.desc: A Navy recruit undergoing officer training has an uneasy romance with a factory worker. He also attracts the attention of a tough, uncompromising sergeant, who sees his potential and is determined to push him to the limit. Romantic drama, starring Richard Gere, Debra Winger, Louis Gossett Jr, Robert Loggia, David Keith and David Caruso(n)

programme.title: Inside the World's Greatest Hotels
programme.desc: The Beverly Hills Hotel, which sits above Sunset Boulevard in Los Angeles. Also known as the Pink Palace, it has been greeting guests for 107 years, from reclusive billionaires to Hollywood royalty. The hotel has seen secrets and scandal, and been the place for countless movie moguls to cut a deal. Director of global guest relations Steven Boggs takes cameras 'backstage', while executive chef Michael Santoro shows the dish that is almost as famous as the hotel itself(n)

programme.title: Secret Scotland
programme.desc: As her journey around her homeland continues, Susan visits the Isle of Skye, where she embarks on an exclusive tour of Dunvegan Castle, which has been home to the McLeods for over 800 years. There, she learns all about clan warfare and discovers a ceremonial horn that has been passed down through generations of clan chiefs since the 14th century. Next, the comedian takes to the seas to operate the world's only turntable ferry, before trying her hand at the accordion. Finally, Susan gains an insight into the workings of a modern-day croft and finds out how to shear a sheep(n)

programme.title: Shock Docs
programme.desc: Documentary exploring some of Britain's most bizarre parent-child relationships, where the mums and dads will do anything to look better than their kids. Those featured include 39-year-old Georgina, who was once a frumpy mum but has now followed daughter Kayla by becoming the image of a Barbie doll, complete with fake boobs and lips. Forty-five-year-old Paul is constantly muscling in on the action with his 18-year-old bodybuilder son Kyle, and former glamour model Charmaine loves flaunting what she has, much to the embarrassment of 14-year-old Yasmin. But when she decides to go back to modelling, how will her daughter react?(n)

programme.title: House Doctor

programme.desc: Documentary profiling youngsters who perform in the big top, offering an honest insight into their closely guarded world. A 17-year-old is sent to the circus as a punishment after getting into trouble with the police, and tries to perfect the knife-throwing act made famous by his father. An eight-year-old makes his debut as a clown alongside his dad, although there's bad news - he may soon have to leave the big top to attend school. As a response, she has decided to buy a property near the historic harbour town of Paphos, in Cyprus. Enlisting the help of Bev's best friend Shelley, Laura Hamilton delivers five potential homes for her budget of £90,000, in the hopes that one of them will fit the bill(n)

programme.desc: The experts head to east London, where properties often sell in days, at open viewings, and for well over the asking price. Phil Spencer meets Katy and Victoria, who have been looking for their first home in the popular area of Walthamstow for almost a year. Three miles away in Leytonstone, Kirstie Allsopp helps friends Mike and Jamie decide between space and the commute to work as their top priority(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer meets expectant parents Steve and Sally, who are relocating to the countryside around Stratford-upon-Avon in Warwickshire. They want to find their dream family home before the baby arrives, but with nearby rural walks, office space and a good village pub on the wish list, Phil has to act fast. Kirstie Allsopp is in Northamptonshire helping Rinku and Sunder. They have been living in a family owned property for the past three years, enabling them to save to buy a place together. However, there are new plans for the land and the bulldozers are inbound, so it's Kirstie's challenge to get the couple into their own home quickly(n)

A power cut threatens to scupper Louise's night before it's even started, until her husband - and farmer - Roddy and his generator come to the rescue. programme.title: David Jason's Great British Inventions
programme.desc: The actor examines British innovations in the history of engineering. programme.title: Holocaust: Night Will Fall (2014)
programme.desc: In 1945, the Ministry of Information's Sidney Bernstein was commissioned to make a documentary that would provide undeniable evidence of the Nazis' crimes. He assembled a team of film-makers including Alfred Hitchcock to record the true horror of the concentration camps, but despite initial government support, the project was deemed too politically sensitive and never broadcast. Now, 70 years later, Bernstein's film has been restored and completed by the Imperial War Museum, and this programme tells the story of its creation. Narrated by Helena Bonham Carter(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition of the documentary features stories of human compassion and kindness in the most challenging circumstances. Allen, 82, has injured his chest after a dizzy spell caused him to fall down 13 steps. As son Graham waits nervously for doctors to assess Allen's injuries, Graham describes his dad's strength and resilience in carrying the family through dark times, and how precious the bond between father and son can be(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Brooke is rushed to St George's after suffering an asthma attack so severe that the paramedic team had to put her in an induced coma at the scene. Doctors are concerned that she may have dangerous amounts of fluid on her lungs and decide to take urgent chest X-rays. Howard, who's 76, arrives with burns to his face, hands and airways after trying to put out an explosion in his garden shed. As his worried wife waits for news in the relatives' room, doctors attempt to stabilise his condition and investigate the extent of his injuries(n)

As son Graham waits nervously for doctors to assess Allen's injuries, Graham describes his dad's strength and resilience in carrying the family through dark times, and how precious the bond between father and son can be(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A look at the specialist team dedicated to caring for people when they suffer the potentially devastating consequences of a stroke, including 77-year-old Alex. Husband Paul sits nervously at her bedside as they wait to see if the drug she has been given will bring back her speech and movement. Meanwhile, doctors examine 66-year-old Steven, who has been admitted with severe stomach cramps(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to Japan on the trail of tofu, wondering how green soy beans are turned into something so white and creamy. Jimmy Doherty investigates reports that supermarket ready meals have nearly doubled in size since the 1950s. He tracks down the ready meals of his childhood and discovers whether bigger plate sizes could also be causing people to eat more. Plus, in Wales, Matt Tebbutt looks into a new kind of milk, which it is claimed is easier to digest(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Anne Hunt from Cheshire has recently retired and has long dreamed of buying a property in Spain where she can spend her new-found free time relaxing in the sun. With her best friend Sharon along to help her with the decision, Danni Menzies shows her five properties on the southern Costa Blanca for her £50,000 budget(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Guy and Julie Sadler from Brighton have travelled all around the world together and have long dreamed of relocating somewhere warm. Now, although they've never been to Spain, they've decided that Almería has the perfect balance of sunshine and affordable property. Laura Hamilton shows them five homes for their £100,000 budget(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers are tested to the limit in pastry week, including an ambitious final challenge that sees them pushed to the edge creating a majestic pie fit for a banquet. Before that, the Signature challenge centres on samosas, with a sweet and a savoury option required, and there is a classic French Technical involving two types of pastry. Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith decide which of the bakers has to leave the competition, with Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig on hand for some emotional support and encouragement(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo and Henry Cole visit a motorbike fan, making table lamps from engines and turning a pallet and iron wheels into a stylish modern coffee table(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head east to Newark in search of their latest upcycling prospects. Simon finds an antique European threshing board, which he turns into a coffee table, and Henry's all at sea with a ship's porthole(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Claire Hilton, who runs Robertsbrook Guest House in Swanage, Dorset, feels she has a good chance of winning the title of best B&B as another round of the competition gets under way. However, guests Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome have doubts over the cleanliness of their bedroom, and another couple are accustomed to more opulent surroundings(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lynn Self and Ziggy Broome hope to impress with their two-star B&B Eden Lodge in Falmouth, Cornwall. However, the state of their property disappoints their fellow competitors. At dinner, James and Maria Church reveal the truth about their famous daughter Charlotte, and the following morning's breakfast reduces some of the guests to fits of giggles(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It is the turn of Charlotte Church's parents James and Maria to play host at Dexby Townhouse in Cardiff, and they pull out all the stops by laying on a day of white-water rafting and a magic performance courtesy of their nephew Elliot. But the following morning, some of the guests are kept waiting for breakfast(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Newlyweds Pip Easby and Nicky Lesser-Easby, who run Muddifords Court Country House in Devon, open their doors for the final stay of the competition. The Churches find their four-poster bed too small, and not all the guests enjoy their trip to a local glass-blowing factory, but feedback at check-out time leaves the hosts feeling confident(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. An underpayment from a surprising source changes the dynamics of the group and throws the competition wide open, before the winner is announced(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: An elephant calf is born at Chester Zoo, but its young half-sister doesn't welcome the new arrival. Meanwhile, Bush dog dad Franco is under pressure as its family grows, and an onager - a rare type of wild horse - faces a major operation(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Andrew Winter and Neale Whitaker help a Brisbane couple, with Ryan having enough of the constant maintenance required by their property(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw and master mechanic Fuzz Townshend travel to Lincoln to renovate a Ford Cortina GT. However, when they arrive at the lock-up garage where it is kept, they find the vehicle has seen much better days, and is now little more than a well-preserved body shell with numerous mechanical parts missing(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Angel Adoree insists that Dick Strawbridge smash a hole through one of the castle's three-foot-thick interior walls to create a honeymoon suite, but he has far too much to do on his own and the schedule slips, badly(n)

programme.title: Auschwitz Untold: In Colour
programme.desc: Part two of two. First-hand accounts from survivors of their fateful arrival at Auschwitz-Birkenau, through to liberation day and its immediate psychological aftermath(n)

programme.title: Holocaust: The Revenge Plot
programme.desc: Documentary, making use of previously undisclosed recordings and candid interviews to tell the story of the Avengers, a secret organisation of Holocaust survivors who came up with a brutal plot in 1946 to seek vengeance on behalf of the six million Jews killed by Hitler and the Nazis. In the dying days of the Second World War, 'warrior-poet' Abba Kovner came to the conclusion that the prosecution of a handful of Nazis would not amount to justice, and instead claimed only 'an eye for an eye' could possibly redress the balance. With the help of a band of like-minded individuals, Kovner planned to murder six million Germans by infiltrating cities and poisoning water supplies. This film also showcases a clandestine recording, made in 1985 at the dying Kovner's request, that identifies all the leading conspirators and how they carried out their operations(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Two families come to terms with life-changing events, including the relatives of eight-year-old Junior, who has an open leg fracture and suspected internal bleeding after being knocked off his bike by a car. His mum Bekki reveals how Junior learned to cope with extraordinary setbacks and how she worries about his future because of health problems of her own. Meanwhile, 46-year-old Anthony arrives in A&E after suffering chest pains during a day out at the races. As doctors attempt to get to the bottom of what's causing his dangerously high heart rate, his wife discusses how Anthony's awareness of his mortality was increased by losing his father(n)

programme.title: The Hurting
programme.desc: Clip show featuring the voice of Jake Yapp, as home videos reveal the dumbest things attempted by adults, kids and even animals(n)

programme.title: Top Gear

Plus, three high-performance hatchbacks are driven with gusto around the test track, and Formula One driver Lewis Hamilton gets a second chance to smash the lap record(n)

programme.title: Cops UK: Bodycam Squad
programme.desc: Keith engages in a high-speed pursuit after spotting a car being driven with a flat tyre. Even after the tyre disintegrates at 90mph, the driver still refuses to stop(n)

programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond try to solve the problems that increasingly complex cars pose to an ageing population. Meanwhile, James May gets acquainted with a revamped Range Rover in London before pitting the luxurious off-roader against an autonomous military machine in Nevada. Actor James McAvoy tackles the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Scrapyard Supercar

This time, they take on the iconic Land Rover Discovery in an off-road hill climb. To defeat such a thoroughbred, the teams need to demonstrate their innovation and imagination(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee Mack's not having much luck with lights these days, whether it's the fizzling sign at the start of this show, the blackout during his recent Sheffield gig, or the dimly lit area at the start of this seventh series. Lee and Lucy have enjoyed a spot of culture at their local cinema, but the sparkle of a French movie is soon diminished when the romantic walk home finds them in an imposing underpass. She has her handbag stolen by a bunch of cocky youths from under his nose. Given the fact the layabout failed to protect her, or come up with an accurate description of their muggers, Lee feels the urge to prove his manliness, but will a spell at the local boxing ring be a help or a hindrance? Sally Bretton and Katy Wix also star(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: A wealthy new couple move into the block and soon find out that sharing a building with Lee and Lucy is anything but straightforward. The fun begins when Lee somehow manages to bag an invitation to a christening party being thrown by the couple - sending him and Lucy on a last-minute gift-buying trip, during which they make every wrong decision humanly possible to find the perfect present for the baby who seems to have everything. Hugh Dennis and Abigail Cruttenden join the cast, with Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Katy Wix(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy decides she wants to have a baby, but what she didn't expect was for the whole family to take a personal interest in the conception. Lee thinks he should be the one, of course - so he can't believe it when she reveals she is planning to have the child with another man. Lee Mack and Sally Bretton star, with Katy Wix, Bobby Ball, Geoffrey Whitehead and Deborah Grant(n)

programme.desc: The three petrolheads demonstrate their love of small cars with a trip to Ukraine. Jeremy Clarkson chooses a Volkswagen Up for the journey, while Richard Hammond takes a Ford Fiesta and James May opts for a Dacia Sandero. On their adventure, the trio absorb the history of the Crimean Peninsula, including a visit to an old submarine pen - but the carefree exploration looks likely to become a chore when Jeremy, Richard and James are challenged to a long drive north through the expansive country, during which they must combat serious boredom. And as if that wasn't arduous enough, a new task is set when they arrive in Kiev - a trip into the wasteland of Chernobyl. programme.desc: Questions regarding the authenticity of the Jehoash Tablet, a religious artifact containing an ancient inscription(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Bible
programme.desc: Egyptologist David Rohl searches for the location of the Tower of Babel, a Biblical structure which first appeared in the Book of Genesis(n)

programme.title: Royal Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Calls to reinvestigate the suspicious death of King Ludwig of Bavaria in 1886, who died three days after he was declared insane and deposed from the throne(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians reveal how the railways transformed the British diet, rescuing a nation that was struggling to feed itself. Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn discover how the mass transportation of livestock gave birth to the traditional British roast, and Ruth Goodman is in Whitby, which became a thriving fishing town thanks to the North Yorkshire Moors Railway supplying its kippers. Plus, Hampshire's watercress line, which brought nutritional salad to the masses, and how the use of steam-powered engines revolutionised production at Britain's oldest brewery(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians take a trip on the Flying Scotsman, arguably the most famous locomotive in the world, along its original route connecting London and Edinburgh - the two most important financial capitals of the Empire. Peter Ginn explores how the railways revolutionised the delivery of mail across Britain, he and Alex Langlands learn about the first electronic communication service - more than 100 years before the arrival of email - and Ruth finds out about the role of the wheeltapper. Plus, why do some railway clocks have more than one minute hand?(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians explore the life of the South Devon branch line before the Beeching cuts of the 1960s. Peter Ginn joins a footplate crew and receives coal-shovelling tips from a veteran steam engine driver of 63 years' experience, and with Alex he learns about one of Scotland's most lucrative exports. Ruth Goodman finds out about the dangers involved in working on the Victorian railways, and how those who lost limbs were catered for by a dedicated prosthetics workshop. She also discovers how the railways came to the rescue when a deadly disease wiped out almost the entire stock of London cattle(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The team discover how the combination of increased leisure time and affordable rail transport brought a new kind of freedom for working-class Victorians. Ruth Goodman travels along the south Devon coast from Paignton to Kingswear, where she helps prepare a paddle steamer for a journey up the River Dart, while at Swanage, Peter Ginn finds out what it was like to work on the excursion trains and the impact mass tourism had on the area(n)

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: High-definition cameras and aerial photography techniques are used to explore the archipelago, which is rich in wildlife and made famous by Charles Darwin's On the Origin of the Species. This programme focuses on the geological history of the islands through the evidence of volcanic eruptions and examines what makes it unique. Tilda Swinton narrates(n)

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: Examining the lesser-known parts of the islands and the species that have successfully evolved to suit the environment. The programme discovers how tortoise shells have changed shape, flowers have turned yellow to attract bees, and finches have become warblers. Plus, how flightless cormorants are still hunting underwater. Narrated by Tilda Swinton(n)

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: An insight into how creatures adapt to the islands' extremes of weather, including iguanas that climb more than 1,000 metres to lay eggs, fish forced to rely on the shade provided by mangroves, and seals that survive on exposed shorelines by taking shelter in lava grottos(n)

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: High-definition cameras and aerial photography techniques are used to explore the archipelago, which is rich in wildlife and made famous by Charles Darwin's On the Origin of the Species. programme.desc: The Garners' next customer in Almería is 76-year-old Sylvia, from Worcestershire, who had planned on moving to Spain with her husband until he died a few years ago. It's Sharon and Steve's job to help her fulfil her dream, but Sylvia is a woman who knows exactly what she wants, and that includes a single-storey property, close to amenities, with a large kitchen, separate dining room, and cream floor tiles. In Alicante, the Rodriguez family looks for a permanent home for lovebirds Adele and Phil. For this green-fingered couple, outside space is a top priority for their 250,000 euro budget(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: A couple want to turn their 20-room chateau into a B&B and yoga retreat, while two musicians start renovating their final untouched room - the old library(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Amy and Marc create an eco-friendly sewage system by connecting the chateau's plumbing to two natural waste ponds 100 metres away. Ben and Billie go fossil hunting in their grounds(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Rebecca struggle to get their driveway ready for the arrival of their guests. Meanwhile, Amy and Marc unearth an original medieval fireplace(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Tim and Rebecca renovate their kitchen, but with a limited budget, they need a bit of creative know-how. Ben and Billie aim to revamp an old outbuilding into a Moroccan oasis(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: It's breaking point for Tim and Rebecca as they struggle to complete their kitchen as an expensive mishap with granite worktops put Rebecca's dream room in jeopardy. Meanwhile, at Ben and Billie's chateau, the duo welcome 60 guests for the wedding of Billie's brother Pete(n)

programme.title: Crazy Delicious
programme.desc: Jayde Adams presents the culinary contest in which inventive cooks are challenged to come up with original takes on classic meals and impress judges Heston Blumenthal, Niklas Ekstedt and Carla Hall. The three contestants in the first episode must come up with a strawberry-based dish and a new take on the hot dog, before one of them is eliminated and the remaining two assigned to create a special birthday meal(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: The return of the documentary following the work of emergency services in Northamptonshire, with this edition focusing on the policing of roads in the county, which has borne more than its fair share of funding cuts. This has resulted in fewer police officers available to attend a huge range of traffic issues, from the routine to the surreal, on the county's 3,000-mile road network, as well as a spike in crime(n)

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson hosts the show in which potential dates bare all bit by bit in a bid to prove they are the one. She is joined by 25-year-old estate agent Hollie-Anna from Margate and 29-year-old Jaison from Norwich, a transsexual man who feels like he has exhausted his local dating pool. They will both choose a date after seeing what their potential partners have to offer on a purely physical level. But will they prove to be a good match?(n)

programme.title: Meet the Drug Lords: Inside the Real Narcos
programme.desc: Former special forces soldier Jason Fox travels through Latin America, investigating the cartel strongholds of Mexico, Colombia and Peru to lift the lid on their brutal worlds. What kind of lives do their members have, what does their work involve and how do they continue to evade the law? Jason begins in Mexico, where he meets members of the Sinaloa cartel - including killers and ex-assassins - witnesses brutality first hand and finds himself in the middle of a turf war as he tries to get to the bottom of how they operate(n)

programme.title: Diamond Dealers and Cockney Geezers
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at Trotters Jewellers in Bethnal Green in London's East End, a family-run business the ownership of which has recently passed to 26-year-old Judd, who manages with old schoolmates Alex and Kallum. The film shows the team designing a commemorative piece for a current world champion boxer, while a bricklayer splashes out on a £9,000 watch, a man buys a diamond necklace to say sorry. Plus, staff travel to New York to get ahead of the trends and meet jeweller to the stars Richie Rich(n)

programme.title: The Royal House of Windsor
programme.desc: The fifth installment of the series focuses on the life and tragic death of Diana, Princess of Wales. Her huge personality and celebrity status challenged the Windsors, culminating in the famous 1995 Panorama interview that prompted the Queen to demand Prince Charles to divorce Diana. After her tragic death, the House of Windsor was shaken, but she left an enduring legacy in the form of her two sons(n)

programme.title: Bone Detectives: Britain's Buried Secrets
programme.desc: Tori Herridge and a team of scientists attempt to piece together the lives behind discovered bones, and find out what happened to these people and how they died. This episode comes from Andover, Hampshire, where the construction of a proposed car park outside the city has been help up by the discovery of remains from over 100 young men. The bones are discovered to date back to Anglo-Saxon times, but appear to have been left in a mass grave rather than granted the Christian burial that was normal for their era(n)

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.desc: Xand van Tulleken and Helen Lawal oversee proceedings as three more pairs road test some of the most popular and talked about diets. Newlyweds Steven and Eamonn have eight days to trim down ahead of their holiday, friends Valetta and Paulette go on six-week diets to slim before their friend's wedding in the sun, and drinking buddies Sian and Keighlee want to lose weight for a girls' holiday in four months' time. Xand investigates whether people's hormones can make them fat, and Helen examines whether knitting can lead to weight loss(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at the School House B&B in Pencader, Carmarthenshire, where owners Jayne and Ian Hall-Edwards hope their environmental approach will win over their guests(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples, Jacqueline and Stephen, Jess and Fred, and Rob and Simon, host dinner parties in Somerset for the chance to win a cash prize of £1,000(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Hull resident Peter and his mum Greta employ the skills of Jonnie Irwin to help find him his dream property in Huelva in Spain on a £150,000 budget(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Shania and Farrukh are on the hunt for the perfect family property in Florida. Ben Hillman helps by lining up five options, hoping one will become their dream holiday home(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton goes house-hunting on the Greek island of Corfu with Simon and Andrea from Manchester, who want a holiday home to share with their sons and grandsons(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef tries to transform the fortunes of Sebastian's, a pizzeria in Burbank, Los Angeles, which is failing to attract customers, leaving the owner in debt and struggling to support his family. Gordon is horrified to discover pre-processed and frozen ingredients on the menu, but there seems to be little he can do to help when he is thrown out of the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Rosaria bought Bella Luna to realise her son Gianfranco's ambition to become a chef, but the Italian restaurant's future looks bleak due to his lack of experience in the kitchen combined with her refusal to acknowledge the eatery's many flaws. Gordon Ramsay heads to Easton, Pennsylvania, to help the troubled pair and save the business from financial disaster(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Boston's La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to floundering soul food restaurant Blackberry's in Plainfield, New Jersey, but struggles to get his message across to indomitable owner Shelley. Her refusal to deal with the truth comes to a head when Gordon finds a dead mouse in the doorway, prompting him to step up his efforts to change her ways(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in New Jersey to help Rose Leone, who spent two years in a coma and came out of hospital to learn her son Michael had run her once-thriving Italian restaurant into the ground. With a menu of more than 126 dishes, the quality of the food is suffering, and even the chefs refuse to eat their own creations(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Junior doctor Silas works his first ever shift in resus, and is part of the team brought in to treat a biker who is rushed to A&E after riding into a lamppost. programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George returns to a grand Victorian villa in Ormskirk to follow the next stage of its renovation. Having previously created a luxurious master en suite, his next task is to tackle the giant kitchen and utility area. He also visits a typical Victorian mansion block in London. Although structurally sound, the flat has been virtually untouched over the years. The result is dismal and depressing with a spare room that feels like a prison cell. Can George mastermind a plan to free up this criminal waste of space?(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The debt collection agents grapple with emotional evictions and moral dilemmas during the festive period, including a young family in Crystal Palace who are made homeless when the landlord repossesses their home because he wants to redevelop the property. Plus, an acrimonious divorce in Chessington comes to a head when the estranged wife obtains a writ to reclaim the house, and an off-licence owner in Hampshire with unpaid rent of more than £5,000 battles to keep his business open just as everything shuts down for the holidays(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

Drama, starring Mark Harmon(n)

programme.desc: A gymnast is murdered, prompting the team to look into her high-pressure lifestyle for clues about the killer. Abby detects malware in the platform's computer systems, while Ducky finds traces on the victim's body that lead to a lawyer who works for the oil company. programme.desc: Gibbs is forced to take a step back from work when his increasingly frail father Jackson begs him to help track down an old friend from the Second World War who does not have long left to live. Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: An exceptional teacher who helped inner-city kids get into college is murdered, but Stabler and Benson's investigation reveals her sinister double life as a sex addict - which has made her plenty of enemies. Crime drama, starring Melina Kanakaredes and Robert Joy(n)

programme.desc: Two room-mates are found bludgeoned to death in their flat, and the handyman is the prime suspect. Award-winning police drama, guest starring Gene Silvers, and Elisabeth Rohm(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica persuades Seth to visit his estranged brother, who promptly turns up dead. The sleuth must uncover whether he was killed by a curse supposedly placed on a stolen Indian pendant, which a diplomat is determined to see returned to its own country. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill(n)

programme.desc: Benson and Stabler try to solve the rape of a veteran police officer, but their efforts are thwarted by officers from the Department of Internal Affairs who seem determined to suppress evidence and bury the case. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni(n)

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong(n)

programme.title: Daddy Daycare
programme.desc: Traditionally minded new father PJ hopes his skills as a management consultant will serve him well when he is sent to spend a week working at Tadpoles Nursery in Chelsea, west London, but his idea of arranging a football lesson for the youngsters ends in chaos. Meanwhile, gay budding parent Richard manages to lose track of two of his charges in an art gallery, and 21-year-old first-time dad Kadmiel gets off to a shaky start when placed in charge of snack time and reading practice for the nursery's four-year-olds(n)

programme.title: Tightrope to Terror (1983)
programme.desc: Four children on a camping holiday in Italy are involved in a dangerous cable car accident that leaves them dangling 150 feet above a glacier. Children's adventure, starring Richard Owens and Mark Jefferis(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours for locals and tourists, exploring Elephant & Castle and Croydon. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay(n)

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show
programme.desc: The Rolling Stones guitarist and former Faces bandmate Ian McLagan reminisce about their time on the road together in the 1970s, and discuss the band's 2011 reunion tour(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Dr Chris Van Tulleken runs a groundbreaking experiment with the University of Surrey to explore whether changing mealtimes can promote weight loss, while Gabriel Weston looks at whether diet products can cause people to put weight on. Plus, an investigation into the chemicals sprayed around houses, the truth about alcohol, and the story of a 12-year-old girl undergoing a new treatment for a peanut allergy(n)

programme.title: Macbeth (1997)
programme.desc: A general in medieval Scotland is told by three witches that he is destined to become king, so plots to murder the current monarch and seize the throne. However, once he has taken the crown he is consumed by paranoia, and goes to terrible lengths to ensure his reign continues. Shakespeare's tragedy, starring Jason Connery and Helen Baxendale(n)

programme.title: The Wind of Change (1961)
programme.desc: An unemployed teenager in Notting Hill grows increasingly frustrated with his life, his anger manifesting as a deep-seated racism. When he and his friends commit a brutal act of violence on a black man and his white girlfriend, the attack has devastating repercussions for his family. Drama, starring Donald Pleasence and Johnny Briggs(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Soho and Milwall, with fascinating facts for locals and tourists alike. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay(n)

programme.title: Spotlight London
programme.desc: Interviews about arts and events showcasing London's vibrant culture scene(n)

programme.title: May to December
programme.desc: Zoe's divorce comes through, and while Alec is keen to celebrate, she is not so sure(n)

programme.title: May to December
programme.desc: Alec gets on so well with Zoe's parents that she begins to feel left out(n)

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams reveals how as Hitler's plans to invade Britain took shape, members of Churchill's Resistance Movement got ready to defend the nation, consulting their specially-prepared instruction manuals on explosives and killing techniques(n)

programme.title: The Repo Man
programme.desc: Part one of two. In recession-hit Britain, many people have turned to finance companies to purchase items over a fixed period of time. This documentary follows the life and work of bailiff Sean James as he confronts debtors across the nation who fail to keep up their repayments. Supported by a rotating crew of part-time agents, including former world-champion boxer Marcus, ex-Marine 'H' and scaffolder Reece, Sean is persistent and has no intention of leaving empty-handed when he tracks down his targets(n)

programme.title: Urban Hymn (2015)
programme.desc: A teenager is encouraged by her social worker to use singing as an escape route from her troubled personal life. She soon finds her loyalties divided between the fresh start music provides for her and the influence of her possessive and volatile best friend. Drama, starring Letitia Wright, Shirley Henderson and Ian Hart(n)

programme.title: One Night in Powder (2015)
programme.desc: A clueless music promoter hits the streets of East London, hoping to put together the greatest club night the capital has ever seen, and recruiting the most eccentric residents of Dalston to help him. Documentary-style comedy, starring Jason Attar(n)

programme.title: Nuremberg - Nazis on Trial
programme.desc: Dramatisation of the trial of Luftwaffe chief and designated successor to Hitler, Hermann Goering. The larger-than-life character was renowned for his charisma and flamboyance, but also as one of the most unrepentant and ruthless of all Nazis. Having aimed to use the courtroom as a means of rehabilitating the fascist regime, it was only at a late stage that he was forced to concede defeat. Robert Pugh stars(n)

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement men who parachuted with the SAS into France on D-Day and the massacre of 35 men in a French woodland(n)

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: As the destruction caused by Hitler's V1s in London and the south of England reached critical proportions, a new fighter was introduced to challenge the enemy's flying bombs. Wing Commander 'Bee' Beaumont remembers his first encounters with the Tempest, and the Wilkinson family from Croydon share their account of life on the ground with help from their day-to-day diary(n)

programme.title: What Next? (1974)
programme.desc: A boy is knocked unconscious by a blow to the head, and when he comes round, he discovers he has gained the power to foretell when a crime is about to be committed. He uses his newfound abilities to help the police bring a criminal mastermind to justice. Children's fantasy adventure, starring Peter Robinson and Perry Benson(n)

programme.desc: Dr David Starkey examines how the Tudor monarch forged a fiercely independent England whose coastal defences and naval power created a new sense of national destiny. He also explores the death of Jane Seymour and her replacement in the monarch's affections by Katherine Howard, whose adultery made her another victim of court intrigue(n)

programme.title: Meet the Lords
programme.desc: Peers turn out in huge numbers to have their say on the most important issue in decades and decide whether to rebel on Brexit or not, while there are ermine gowns to put on and ancient traditions to follow for the day of the Queen's speech - the biggest ceremonial event of the year. Plans to overhaul Parliament's Victorian sewage system could mean a move for the five or so years the work will take, and Baroness D'Souza has new research about peers who claim expenses without contributing to the House(n)

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.desc: Documentary investigating allegations that a lack of proper controls is allowing financial, physical and even sexual abuse to take place within Britain's mosques. Over a year in the making, this programme exposes how worshippers' generosity can be exploited by religious leaders and how extreme groups can assume control of a community(n)

programme.title: Interview with a Hitman (2012)
programme.desc: An assassin is raised to be the perfect killing machine in the slums of Romania, but when his masters betray him, he fakes his death and tries to start a new life in London. However, he soon finds his dark past coming back to haunt him. Action thriller, starring Luke Goss, Caroline Tillette and Danny Midwinter(n)

programme.title: The Repo Man
programme.desc: Part two of two. Bailiff Sean James repossesses cars belonging to debtors who have taken out high-interest logbook loans and failed to keep up their repayments. During the worst snowfall in 30 years, he spots an opportunity to carry out his work while most people are stranded in their homes, and the programme meets those who fight tooth and nail to keep their vehicles, as well as drivers who have unknowingly bought cars with debt attached(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)
